summary: Audit web app vs mobile (Android) build surface-by-surface to map every parity gap, wiring difference, and PWA/cross-browser issue

plan.executiveSummary: Web is ~9 mobile versions behind (frozen at v1.0.8 while mobile is at v1.0.17) and the gap is not incremental polish — three of the four core surfaces (Log/capture, Insights/Report, Premium, Settings+Onboarding) require ground-up rebuilds, and the entire identity + atmosphere foundation has diverged. The single most consequential issue is that web still runs the removed email/Google sign-in stack while mobile went fully device-ID-only at v1.0.9; because web gates premium/progress/modes fetches behind an `isSignedIn` flag that is now permanently false (nobody can sign in once that surface is the only identity), large parts of the app silently never load. Compounding this, web's `@triggermap/shared` alias points at a STALE LOCAL COPY (`web/shared/`) that is missing `contributions.js` and the entire `knowledge/` library (movements/nourishments) and has divergent `emotions.js`/`tags.js` — so web literally cannot consume the v1.0.16 adaptive-tag and adaptive-modes logic until that is re-pointed at the monorepo root `shared/`. The backend is already 100% device-ID ready (every owner-scoped route resolves ownerId = token?.id || deviceId, and all routes — actions, recover, register-device, push-token, notification-prefs, share, modes/{feedback,profile,regenerate}, subscription/verify — exist), so essentially ALL parity work is web-side. The shape of the work: (1) fix the two foundational substrates (shared-alias + device-ID identity/api wiring) that unblock everything; (2) build the cross-cutting presentation+state primitives many surfaces share (emotional-state provider, atmospheric field, icon tab bar, onboarding state machine + spotlight/tooltip components, EmotionPad, contribution-tag util, emotionColor/emotionModel port); (3) then rebuild each surface (Log, Timeline, Report, Premium, Settings) on top of those primitives; (4) finish with PWA polish and Web Push. Roughly 2 XL rebuilds (Report, For-You modes), an XL onboarding system, an XL EmotionPad/Log flow, plus a long tail of M/L surface rewrites. The only genuinely new product decision (not a port) is the web subscription/purchase path, since web cannot use native Play IAP.
sharedPrereqs: RE-ALIAS SHARED (do first, unblocks everything): delete the stale web/shared/ copy and point web/next.config.js @triggermap/shared alias at the monorepo root ../shared, OR wire the workspace package. Root shared/ has contributions.js + knowledge/ (movementLibrary/Catalogue, nourishmentLibrary/Catalogue) + current emotions.js/tags.js that web/shared/ lacks (confirmed: web/shared/knowledge is empty, no contributions.js). Without this web CANNOT import getContributionSuggestions, MOVEMENTS/NOURISHMENTS, or current emotion derivation — it blocks adaptive tags, For You modes, and emotion-label parity. Rebuild after re-aliasing.

DEVICE-ID IDENTITY REWRITE of web/hooks/useSession.js + web/lib/api.js (the deepest cross-cutting break, every surface depends on it): rewrite useSession to bootstrap on getDeviceId() unconditionally (no token gate), keep token:null/user:null as stable nulls (so existing call sites still compile), call fetchMe(null, deviceId) on every load to hydrate subscription + add firstAiFreeAvailable state, fire-and-forget registerDevice(deviceId), run one-time recover(deviceId, legacyToken) gated by a localStorage 'triggermap.web.recovery-done' flag (reads leftover 'triggermap.web.token' once then clears it), add ensureDeviceIdentity(), and DROP signInWithEmail/registerWithEmail/signInWithGoogle/signOut/isSignedIn. In web/lib/api.js: thread deviceId into fetchMe, editMomentApi, deleteMomentApi, deleteAllDataApi, fetchProgress, fetchModes (append ?deviceId= on GET, merge into body on writes); FIX submitActionFeedback to POST /api/actions (currently posts to non-existent /api/actionFeedback — 404); add wrappers for recover, registerDevice, createShareSnapshot/fetchShareSnapshot, registerPushToken/unregisterPushToken, saveNotificationPrefs/getNotificationPrefs, verifySubscription, submitModeFeedback, fetchModeProfile/updateModeProfile, regeneratePremium, fetchModeOutput; add lang param, _t cache-buster, and in-flight GET dedup; remove loginApi/registerApi.

EMOTION MODEL + COLOR PORT: create web/lib/emotionModel.js porting mobile/utils/emotionModel.js emotionColor(valence,arousal) (continuous ~7-color quadrant/threshold map) and FIELD_GRADIENT, plus resolveEmotion()/momentEmotion() coordinate-fallback (coordinatesToLegacy) so coordinate-only moments are not mis-bucketed as neutral/undefined. This is consumed by the atmosphere, timeline cards/dots, EmotionGarden blooms+centroid, MoodWeather bucketing, and the dominant-emotion computation — a single source of truth must exist before those are rebuilt.

GLOBAL EMOTIONAL-STATE PROVIDER: create web/hooks/useEmotionalState.js (EmotionalStateProvider) porting computeDominantEmotion/Trigger/Trend + EMOTION_PALETTE + SCORE map from mobile, mounted in web/pages/_app.js, reading the same device-ID-scoped timeline web already fetches. It drives the AtmosphericField hue, the tab bar active tint, and emotion-specific prompts/nudges on Log — currently web recomputes a thin valence-only dominant per-page for a single static glow. Many surfaces (atmosphere, log prompts, timeline color) read from this.

ONBOARDING STATE MACHINE + TOUR PRIMITIVES: create web/hooks/useOnboarding.js (OnboardingProvider, forward-only states not_started→framing_shown→first_log_done→timeline_seen→second_log_done→insights_seen→completed, localStorage-backed 'triggermap.onboarding-state' with legacy-flag migration, advance/skip/isPast/markNudgeSeen/isNudgeSeen) + web/components/SpotlightOverlay.js (SpotlightOverlay + GuidedTooltip) + web/components/Tooltip.js (localStorage seen-flags 'triggermap.tooltip.seen.*'), mounted in _app.js. Required by Log, Timeline, and Report FTUE and by the post-log redirect branching — must exist before those surfaces wire their tooltips/nudges.

WEB i18n LAYER (or a deliberate deferral decision): mobile routes every string through t() with en+hi (i18n/en.json, hi.json incl. log.*, emotion.*, triggers.*, emotions.*, ftue.*, nudge.*, report.*, triggerMap.*, premium.*, settings.*, weather.*, onboarding.*). Web has NO i18n directory and hardcodes English everywhere. Decide: stand up a web i18n context + en/hi catalogs now (so all rebuilt surfaces and the Settings Language selector are wired correctly from the start and Hindi parity is real), or explicitly defer hi and at minimum structure copy + copy en strings verbatim. Deferring is cheaper but means re-touching every rebuilt surface later, so the recommendation is to stand up the context early even if hi catalog lags.

DESIGN-SYSTEM PRIMITIVES used by every surface: a reusable web Card (diagonal LinearGradient sheen pseudo-element + top hairline highlight + optional accent left-edge + glow + entrance fade) and PrimaryButton variants (soft-rect radius ~18px, accentStrong solid fill + cyan glow, secondary/danger/outline, scale-0.96 press) reconciled to mobile in web/styles/globals.css; align base font off 'Trebuchet MS' to a system-ui/Roboto stack (or bundle Inter) with the theme.type scale + letter-spacing; introduce CSS custom properties mirroring theme.motion tokens (durations, stagger70, curves) plus count-up + directional-rise utilities. These underlie cards/buttons on Log, Timeline, Report, Premium, Settings.

Add web/pages/_offline.js + wire next-pwa fallbacks {document:'/_offline'} in web/next.config.js; gitignore + untrack the stale committed web/public/sw.js + web/public/workbox-*.js build artifacts.

Add a 180x180 opaque apple-touch-icon (currently reuses 192) + web/pages/_document.js with <html lang>; i18n the InstallModalIOS.js copy; in web/hooks/usePWAInstall.js distinguish iOS Safari from in-app/Chrome WebViews and persist a dismissal flag so the install CTA isn't sticky-true/nagging; surface the install nudge during first-run onboarding.

risks: Web subscription/purchase has NO ported path — web cannot use native Play IAP (subscriptionService.web.js throws by design). This is the only true product decision, not a port: the user must choose between integrating a browser checkout (Stripe/Razorpay/Play web) that verifies against the existing /api/subscription/verify keyed by web deviceId, or deliberately deferring purchase while still reflecting entitlement bought on the same identity. Until decided, the Premium and For-You workstreams cannot fully close, though they can reflect already-purchased entitlement.

Data-recovery for previously-signed-in web users is fragile: web previously had Google/email login, so some web devices ARE linked (deviceUser:{deviceId}→userId via old loginApi({deviceId})) and auto-heal on reads, but devices whose link is missing need the token-driven recover(deviceId, legacyToken) reading the leftover 'triggermap.web.token' exactly once. If that one-shot is mishandled (cleared before result.ok, or run on a deviceId that differs from the one originally sent to loginApi) those users' history stays stranded. Confirm getDeviceId() keeps returning the SAME stored value.

Re-aliasing @triggermap/shared to the monorepo root may surface build/transpile issues (Next pages-router + next-pwa + the root shared/ using ESM/JSX or test files); the root shared/ also contains __tests__ dirs that must not be bundled. A rebuild after re-aliasing is mandatory and may need transpilePackages or include/exclude tuning.

RN-only interaction fidelity will not fully reproduce on web: the EmotionPad's Reanimated UI-thread cursor/trail/glow, expo-haptics, and spring physics map only approximately to pointer events + CSS + navigator.vibrate (which Safari/iOS does not support at all). Set expectations that the web pad is a faithful-but-not-identical reimplementation, and that iOS gets no haptics.

The AtmosphericField's 4 continuously-animating blurred radial blobs at full viewport can be a real performance/battery cost on low-end Android Chrome and especially iOS Safari (large blur filters are expensive); needs prefers-reduced-motion fallback and possibly reduced blob count/blur on small screens.

i18n is a fork in the road: standing up the web i18n context + hi catalog early is more upfront work but avoids re-touching every rebuilt surface; deferring hi risks shipping English-only Hindi parity gaps and a second pass across Log/Report/Premium/Settings. The decision affects estimates across nearly every workstream.

Removing sign-in entirely (deleting login.js, GSI script, loginApi/registerApi) is correct per device-ID memory, but the backend login/register routes remain for backward-compat — ensure no web code path still calls them and that the leftover token is only read during the one-time recovery, never written.

Web Push parity is partial: iOS Safari only supports Web Push for INSTALLED PWAs (16.4+) and not in regular Safari tabs, so 'weekly insights / daily check-in / nudges' delivery on iOS depends on the user having installed the PWA — a delivery gap vs Android that should be communicated and gated in the Settings notifications UI.
plan.sequencing: Do the two foundations first and in this order: (1) re-alias @triggermap/shared to the monorepo root and delete the stale web/shared copy — nothing else can correctly import contributions/knowledge until this lands; then (2) rewrite useSession + web/lib/api.js to the device-ID contract (fix the /api/actionFeedback→/api/actions 404, thread deviceId everywhere, add the ~12 missing wrappers, delete sign-in). These two unblock literally every surface. Next build the cross-cutting primitives in one batch because four+ surfaces share them: emotionModel (color/coordinate-fallback), the EmotionalStateProvider, the AtmosphericField + icon tab bar, the onboarding state machine + SpotlightOverlay/Tooltip components, the adaptiveTags util, the Card/Button/font/motion design primitives, and the i18n context. Only then rebuild surfaces, ordered by dependency and value: Log flow first (P0 — it is where users enter data and where the FTUE begins and feeds the onboarding state the other surfaces read), then the Report Read-spine + early-detection + animated Progress (P0 — the largest single rebuild and the product's headline), with Report's For-You modes as a tightly-coupled follow-on once the Read spine exists. Timeline (P1) can proceed in parallel with Report once primitives land since it mainly consumes emotionModel + onboarding. Premium and Settings/Onboarding (P1) follow, with the web purchase decision pulled forward as a product question because it blocks Premium's CTA. Finish with the P2 tail — Web Push + offline-first localStore (functional hardening for the installed PWA) and the PWA install/manifest/offline-page/icon polish — which depend on the foundation but not on the surface rebuilds and can be slotted whenever capacity allows. Throughout, validate cross-browser (Android Chrome, iOS Safari) after the AtmosphericField, EmotionPad, and tab bar land, since those are the highest-risk-for-perf-and-touch surfaces.

reports.surface: Emotion logging / capture flow (Log tab + emotion-selection + trigger-selection)
reports.mobileBehavior: Two-screen flow. (1) TriggerSelectionScreen (the Log tab) renders: kicker + a state-aware prompt (getPrompt: returns a returning-user prompt at >=3 today, else a dominant-emotion-specific prompt e.g. 'Steady waters...', else a rotating default), a today-count hint line, a persistent value statement ('why' line), a MoodWeather forecast ribbon, a StreakOrb, a state-aware pattern nudge (only when dominantEmotion && momentCount>=3, with trend arrows and trigger/improving/declining/build-patterns body), a 3-wide grid of TriggerTiles (gradient/halo/icon-disc, per-trigger color, spring press + haptic), and a bottom encouragement card whose copy escalates by moments.length/todayCount. All content i18n via t(). Staggered entrance animations. FTUE: a one-time SpotlightOverlay framing card ('🎯', logFirstMoment CTA, skip) on first visit after the onboarding carousel, a GuidedTooltip trigger hint, plus a progressive 'check insights' nudge at momentCount>=5. Tapping a tile routes to /emotion?trigger=<t>. (2) EmotionSelectionScreen: back button, kicker=trigger label, prompt, and the EmotionPad — a gesture-driven 2D valence×arousal pad (drag a cursor; continuous coords quantized to 0.05; magnetic center snap within 0.08; a 5-dot motion trail; axis-crossing flashes; quadrant-change + region-change haptics; a live emotion dot + capitalized derivedEmotionLabel + a human intensity-qualified summary that track the cursor on the UI thread; four axis anchors Intense/Calm/Unpleasant/Pleasant; accent color from emotionColor(valence,arousal)). On first interaction an adaptive 'What contributed?' tag section springs in: chips come from getContributionSuggestions(domain=trigger, valence, arousal, intensity, emotionLabel) ranked by per-(trigger:region) usage history (getRelevantContributionSuggestionsSync), re-animating on every region crossing, max 3 selectable (MAX_TAGS_PER_MOMENT) with a progress-dot row, and a pinned 'Selected' row for chips that leave the live pool. Optional note. Save button is disabled until hasInteracted, colored with accent. On save: builds a rich payload (trigger, valence, arousal, intensity, emotionPoint {valence,arousal,x,y}, emotionLabel, emotionSubtitle, emotionQuadrant, emotionIntensity, note, lang, tags, contributionTags, contributionTagMeta), calls saveMoment, records tag usage, fires success haptic + toast, then shows a post-log scene: animated orb (spring scale + looping glow) with 3 looping ripple rings in the accent color, derivedLabel text, a title that is FTUE-aware ('Your first data point' / 'Patterns forming' / 'Heard you'), a FeedbackCard, optional FTUE subtext, and a 'View on timeline'/'See insights' button. Redirect after 3s is onboarding-aware: first log → /(tabs)/timeline (advance first_log_done), second log → /(tabs)/report (advance second_log_done), steady state → /(tabs)/timeline; hardware back during post-log goes to timeline.
reports.webStatus: web/pages/index.js (frozen at v1.0.8) is a single-page 2-step flow. Step 1 'trigger': a feature card with getPrompt (3 rotating prompts, no emotion-specific prompts), today-count copy, MoodWeather, StreakOrb, a pattern nudge (computeDominant inline, valence-averaged), a tileGrid of plain emoji+label buttons (no gradient/halo/per-trigger color/press animation), and a bottom card with escalating copy. Step 2 'emotion': TWO HTML <input type=range> sliders (Feel/Energy) with FEEL_LABELS/ENERGY_LABELS step labels; coords via createEmotionCoordinates (snapped to 9 EMOTION_AXIS_STEPS); a live summary card showing derivedLabel + 'feel/energy' coords; a STATIC adaptive tag section sourced from REGION_TAGS[region] (no domain awareness, no usage ranking, no pinned row), max 3; a note textarea; a 'Log moment' button. handleSave posts {trigger, valence, arousal, intensity, emotion: coordinatesToLegacy(...), note, notes, tags}. Post-log scene exists (stateGlow, 3 rippleRings, postLogOrb showing derivedLabel text, 'Heard you.' title, FeedbackCard, 'View on timeline' button) and always router.push('/timeline') after 3s. No EmotionPad, no gestures/trail/haptics, no FTUE/onboarding awareness, no i18n, no emotion-specific prompts, no contribution-tag learning, and it sends a much thinner payload via the legacy token-based useSession.

gaps.title: No 2D EmotionPad — web uses two static range sliders instead of the gesture-driven valence×arousal pad
gaps.description: Mobile's core capture surface is EmotionPad.js: a continuous 2D pad with a draggable cursor, magnetic-center snap (0.08 radius), a 5-dot motion trail, axis-crossing flashes, quadrant/region haptics, a live UI-thread-driven label+summary, and an intensity-driven glow. Web replaces this with two HTML range sliders (axisSliderFeel/axisSliderEnergy) that snap to the 9 EMOTION_AXIS_STEPS. The interaction model, feel, and visual language diverge completely; web also lacks the live human summary ('slightly anxious', 'very energized') from humanSummary(). Port a web pointer/touch-driven 2D pad component reproducing cursor + trail + glow + live label/summary; reuse derivedEmotionLabel and a web colorForLabel for the accent. Keep range-slider as an a11y fallback only.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/components/EmotionPad.js (EmotionPad, humanSummary, gesture Pan worklets, useAnimatedReaction live coords); mobile/utils/emotionModel.js (emotionColor)
gaps.webTarget: new web/components/EmotionPad.js; rewrite step 'emotion' block in web/pages/index.js (lines ~259-367); web/styles/globals.css (replace .axisSlider* with pad styles)

gaps.title: Adaptive contribution tags missing — web uses static REGION_TAGS, no domain awareness or usage learning
gaps.description: Mobile builds 'What contributed?' chips with getContributionSuggestions({domain: trigger, valence, arousal, intensity, emotionLabel}) which combine domain-specific tags (work/family/partner/... × 9 regions, with same-valence neighbour top-up) plus region feeling words, then ranks them by per-(trigger:region) usage history via getRelevantContributionSuggestionsSync and persists selections with recordTagUsage. It also re-animates the pool on every region crossing, shows a 3-dot progress row, and pins selected-but-out-of-pool chips in a 'Selected' row. Web only does REGION_TAGS[region] (generic, trigger-agnostic, no learning, no pinned row, no meta). The shared module shared/constants/contributions.js already exists and is web-importable. Port a web adaptiveTags util (localStorage-backed instead of AsyncStorage) and rewrite the web tag section to use getContributionSuggestions + ranking, max MAX_TAGS_PER_MOMENT, with pinned-selected row.
gaps.mobileSource: shared/constants/contributions.js (getContributionSuggestions, buildContributionTagMeta); mobile/utils/adaptiveTags.js (getRelevantContributionSuggestionsSync, recordTagUsage); EmotionSelectionScreen.js tag section (lines ~355-408)
gaps.webTarget: new web/lib/adaptiveTags.js; rewrite tag section + imports in web/pages/index.js (drop REGION_TAGS import, add contributions); web/styles/globals.css (.tagChip pinned row)

gaps.title: Identity/wiring mismatch — web still uses legacy token sign-in instead of device-ID-only model
gaps.description: Mobile (v1.0.9+) is device-ID-only: useAppSession has no token/user, bootstraps getOrCreateDeviceId, registers the device, hydrates premium via fetchMe(null, deviceId), runs offline-first local storage with a server merge, and a pending-sync retry queue. Web useSession.js is still the OLD model — it bootstraps from getStoredToken/fetchMe(token), exposes signInWithEmail/registerWithEmail/signInWithGoogle/signOut, and saveMoment just calls logMomentApi(payload, token) with no local store/merge/retry. web/lib/api.js getDeviceId() exists and logMoment already injects deviceId, but the session layer still threads a token and assumes sign-in. This is a cross-cutting parity break that affects the log flow's saveMoment path and the post-log redirect identity. Align web useSession to device-ID identity (drop token bootstrap/sign-in flows or make them no-ops), and have saveMoment build the same rich payload mobile sends.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js (device-ID bootstrap, saveMoment payload build, loadTimeline merge, pending sync); see memory device-id-identity.md
gaps.webTarget: web/hooks/useSession.js (rewrite to device-ID, remove token/sign-in); web/lib/api.js (logMoment payload); web/pages/_app.js (Google GSI script likely removable)

gaps.title: saveMoment payload is thin — web omits emotionPoint/emotionLabel/emotionQuadrant/emotionIntensity/contributionTags/contributionTagMeta
gaps.description: Mobile EmotionSelectionScreen.handleSave sends trigger, valence, arousal, intensity, emotionPoint {valence,arousal,x,y}, emotionLabel (derivedLabelKey), emotionSubtitle, emotionQuadrant (from contributionSuggestionSet.emotionQuadrant), emotionIntensity (intensityBand), note, lang, tags, contributionTags, contributionTagMeta (buildContributionTagMeta). useAppSession.saveMoment forwards all of these to the backend so server-side report/pattern logic and contribution analytics get full fidelity. Web handleSave sends only {trigger, valence, arousal, intensity, emotion, note, notes, tags}. The backend receives degraded data from web (no contribution meta, no emotionLabel/quadrant/intensity band). Mirror the mobile payload in web handleSave and the web logMoment wrapper.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/screens/EmotionSelectionScreen.js handleSave (lines ~196-218); mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js saveMoment contributionFields (lines ~291-333)
gaps.webTarget: web/pages/index.js handleSave (lines ~126-155); web/hooks/useSession.js saveMoment; web/lib/api.js logMoment

gaps.title: No 'value at first log' / FTUE onboarding awareness in the log flow (v1.0.11)
gaps.description: Mobile gates the log flow on an onboarding state machine (useOnboarding): a one-time SpotlightOverlay framing card with a 'log your first moment' CTA + skip on the Log tab, GuidedTooltip hints ('What just happened?', 'How did it affect you?'), and a progressive 'check your insights' nudge at momentCount>=5. The post-log scene changes its title/subtext for first log ('Your first data point' / firstDataPointSub) and second log ('Patterns forming' / patternsFormingSub) and advances the state. Web has zero onboarding state, no framing overlay, no guided tooltips, no first/second-log copy — every save shows the generic 'Heard you.' This is the entire v1.0.11 'value at first log' surface missing on web.

gaps.title: Post-log redirect is not onboarding-aware — web always goes to /timeline
gaps.description: Mobile redirects after a 3s post-log scene based on onboarding state: first log → /(tabs)/timeline (advance first_log_done), second log → /(tabs)/report (advance second_log_done, button reads 'See insights'), steady state → /(tabs)/timeline; hardware-back during post-log also routes to timeline. Web unconditionally router.push('/timeline') (lines 148 and 176) with a single 'View on timeline' button. Add the second-log → /report branch and FTUE-aware button label once a web onboarding state exists.

gaps.title: No useEmotionalState provider on web — dominant-emotion nudge/prompts use a thinner, valence-only inline computation
gaps.description: Mobile derives dominantEmotion/dominantTrigger/emotionalTrend/momentCount/emotionColor centrally in useEmotionalState (EmotionalStateProvider), used to drive the emotion-specific prompt (getPrompt), the pattern nudge, the bottom card, and the app-wide atmosphere. Crucially momentEmotion() resolves coordinate-only moments via coordinatesToLegacy so new-model moments aren't all bucketed neutral, and the trend uses EMOTION_SCORE incl. derived-label safety nets. Web recomputes a local computeDominant() inline that averages numeric valence only (no SCORE map, no legacy/derived-label fallback) and crucially never produces an emotion-specific prompt (web getPrompt has only 3 generic prompts; mobile has EMOTION_PROMPTS_EN/log.prompts.<emotion>). Port a web useEmotionalState (or at least the SCORE/momentEmotion resolution + emotion-specific prompts) so the prompt, nudge, and color match mobile.

gaps.title: TriggerTile visual parity — web tiles are flat emoji+label buttons
gaps.description: Mobile TriggerTile renders a per-trigger colored tile with a LinearGradient depth fill, a top highlight, a layered luminous halo behind an icon disc with colored border, capitalized label, and a spring press-in scale + haptic. Web triggerTile is a plain button with an emoji span and label (data-trigger attr only); the emoji set also differs slightly and there's no per-trigger color, halo, gradient, or press animation. The TRIGGER_COLORS map exists in web/lib/designSystem.js but is unused by the tile. Rebuild the web tile to match (CSS gradients/box-shadow can stand in for the native halo/elevation).

gaps.title: No haptics, no live-tracking animations, no internationalization on the web log flow
gaps.description: Mobile uses expo-haptics throughout (tap, selection, success, region/quadrant impacts), reanimated-driven trail/glow/label-pop, and routes every string through i18n t() (log.*, emotion.*, triggers.*, emotions.*, ftue.*, nudge.*). Web has none of these: no Web Vibration API usage, no equivalent micro-animations beyond CSS ripple rings, and all copy is hardcoded English (no web i18n directory exists). Add navigator.vibrate fallbacks on key transitions, CSS/JS micro-animations for the pad/tag reveal, and at minimum structure copy for future i18n.

gaps.title: EmotionPad axis anchors / region label / derived 18-label nuance not surfaced on web sliders
gaps.description: Mobile shows axis anchors (Intense/Calm/Unpleasant/Pleasant), a region label (t('emotion.regions.<regionKey>')), and a continuously updating 18-value derivedEmotionLabel with intensity tiers (overwhelmed vs anxious, peaceful vs calm, etc.). Web does compute derivedEmotionLabel and shows it in the summary card, but the slider step labels (Rough/Off/Okay/Good/Great, Drained/Low/Steady/Alert/Wired) flatten the model to 1D-per-axis and there is no region label or anchor framing, so the richer feeling vocabulary is under-communicated. When porting the pad, surface region label + anchors to match.

reports.wiringNotes: Both apps call the same backend /api/logMoment (web via web/lib/api.js logMoment, mobile via mobile/services/api.logMoment) and inject a deviceId. BUT identity wiring diverges: web/hooks/useSession.js still bootstraps a Bearer token (getStoredToken→fetchMe(token)) and exposes email/Google sign-in, while mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js is device-ID-only with NO token/user (matches memory device-id-identity.md). web getDeviceId() uses localStorage key 'triggermap.web.deviceId' (crypto.randomUUID) — fine and distinct from mobile's deviceId, expected per-platform. Payload mismatch: web saveMoment sends only {trigger, valence, arousal, intensity, emotion(=coordinatesToLegacy), note, notes, tags}; mobile additionally sends emotionPoint, emotionLabel, emotionSubtitle, emotionQuadrant, emotionIntensity, contributionTags, contributionTagMeta, lang — so the backend receives degraded contribution/emotion metadata from web. Web has NO offline-first local store, NO server/local merge on loadTimeline, and NO pending-sync retry queue (mobile has all three in useAppSession). The post-log FeedbackCard consumes the same response shape (patternFeedback, smartReflectionPrompt, pairCount) on both, so that contract is aligned. No client analytics on web (mobile trackEvent('moment_logged')). No adaptive-tag usage persistence on web (mobile persists to AsyncStorage; web would use localStorage). Coordinate→feeling resolution (v1.0.12): the shared coordinatesToLegacy fix is used by web on save, but web lacks momentEmotion() coordinate-fallback in its dominant-emotion computation, so coordinate-only moments may still be mis-bucketed in the web nudge/prompt logic.
reports.summary: The web log flow is materially behind: it sits at its v1.0.8 committed state (two static range sliders + static REGION_TAGS + legacy token sign-in), while mobile has the gesture-driven 2D EmotionPad, the domain-aware learning contribution-tag system (shared getContributionSuggestions + per-region usage ranking), full FTUE 'value at first log' onboarding with first/second-log post-log copy and report redirect, and device-ID-only offline-first identity sending a much richer payload. Reaching parity requires building a web EmotionPad, porting the adaptive-tag util to localStorage, rewriting useSession to device-ID identity with a fuller saveMoment payload, and adding a web onboarding state machine. Core animations (ripple rings, post-log orb) and the shared emotion math already exist on web, so the foundation is partly in place but the headline interaction and wiring are not.

reports.mobileBehavior: TimelineScreen (v1.0.17) renders inside a scrolling ScreenShell with edges on all sides. Layout order top-to-bottom: (1) FadeInView header with kicker t('timeline.kicker'), 30px title t('timeline.title'), and a count-aware subtitle (subtitleWithCount/Plural or subtitleEmpty); (2) MoodWeather ribbon fed all moments; (3) a 'emotional trajectory' toggle button (PressableScale, shown only when moments.length >= 2) that expands/collapses; (4) the EmotionTrajectory 2D valence/arousal field (only when toggled AND >=2 moments) — a 300px square plotting last-30-day moments with quadrant grid, 4 corner zone labels (Stressed/Energized/Low/Calm) where the dominant zone is emphasized, axis edge labels, a dashed baseline 'usual centre' ring, recency-faded connecting trail lines, recency-colored/sized dots (newest is largest with white border + colored shadow), an animated pulsing halo on the newest point, tappable dots that Alert the moment detail, a Sparkline of valence over the window, a CountUpText micro-summary ('N moments · mostly <zone> · now <drift>'), and a legend line; (5) EmotionGarden (with highlightEmotion driven by which moment is being edited); (6) a Tooltip (hidden when micro-insights exist or during first-log FTUE); (7) three GuidedTooltip FTUE/nudge overlays — first-log timeline explain, then patterns-over-time, then a 'deeper patterns' progressive nudge that fires once at 10+ moments via useOnboarding markNudgeSeen/isNudgeSeen; (8) dynamic MicroInsight list (generateMicroInsights); (9) error state card with retry; (10) day-grouped sections. Grouping: moments are first run through mergeSimilarMoments (collapses same trigger + resolved-emotion within a 30-min window into one entry carrying _count and _grouped, keeping earliest timestamp and merging notes; uses resolveEmotion() which falls back from m.emotion to coordinatesToLegacy(valence,arousal) so coordinate-only moments don't merge as undefined===undefined), then groupByDay using getRelativeDayLabel which yields 'Today'/'Yesterday'/localized 'weekday, day month' (en-IN/hi-IN). Each day section is a FadeInView with staggered delay, an uppercase dayHeader with bottom border, and a vertical timeline-connector column: each item has a connector dot colored by emotion + a connector line to the next item (color at 40/30% alpha), and the card itself is wrapped in an Animated.View that, for the newest moment on load, animates a 3-second fade-out emotion-colored border + shadow highlight (highlightAnim). The card is TimelineGroup: a glass card with a 3px left border in the emotion color, LinearGradient sheen + top hairline highlight, a trigger-colored icon disc with halo (TRIGGER_COLORS + TRIGGER_ICONS emoji), trigger label (i18n triggers.*), an emotion badge (tinted bg in emotion color, EMOTION_ICONS emoji + capitalized translated/derived label), an optional ×N count badge, a 2-line note, a contribution-tags row (uses moment.contributionTags||tags, shows up to 3 + '+N' overflow pill), a right-aligned localized time, and an actions row (Edit ✏️ / Delete 🗑️) separated by a top border; delete triggers warning haptic + native confirm Alert. Emotion color is computed from valence/arousal via emotionModel.emotionColor when coordinates exist, else from a 5-key legacy EMOTION_COLORS map. Editing opens EditMomentModal: a bottom-sheet Modal (slide, safe-area aware) with a Trigger chip grid (all TRIGGERS), an Emotion chip grid (all EMOTIONS), a multiline note input, and Cancel/Save; Save calls updateMoment(id, {trigger, emotion, note}) then reloads. Empty state (no moments, not loading, no error) is a centered FadeInView with 📝, t('timeline.emptyTitle'), t('timeline.emptyBody'), and a 'Log a moment' PrimaryButton routing to /(tabs)/log. The 'View on timeline' deep-link is a post-log redirect (EmotionSelectionScreen router.replace('/(tabs)/timeline')) — there is no query param; the highlight is purely the newest-moment-on-load animation. Loading is handled by ScreenShell with i18n loading/timeout copy and retry.
reports.webStatus: web/pages/timeline.js (v1.0.8) renders inside Layout titled 'Timeline' with a Refresh ghost button. Order: a state-adaptive stateGlow div (computes dominant recent-24h emotion calm/neutral/frustrated from valence/legacy and tints a glow); a cardFeature header ('Past 7 days' kicker, 'Timeline' h2, '<N> moments this week' or 'Your moments, grouped by day.'); MoodWeather; EmotionGarden (no highlight prop); dynamic MicroInsight list; a plain loadingCard div while loading; an error feedback panel with Try again; an empty-state panel (📝, 'No moments yet', copy, 'Log a moment' link to '/'); then day-grouped sections. Grouping: mergeSimilarMoments collapses same trigger + RAW m.emotion within 30 min (only sets _count, no _grouped, no resolveEmotion fallback); groupByDay uses date.toLocaleDateString('en-IN', {weekday,day,month}) — NO Today/Yesterday relative labels, always English locale. Each day header shows the date + a 'N moments' count chip (mobile has no per-day count chip). Items render in a tlConnector with tlDot (emotion color + glow) and tlLine; cards are an article.momentCard with a 3px colored left border and, for the newest moment, a tlCardNewest class + --glow-color (CSS-driven newest highlight, roughly matching the 3s glow). Card content: a momentMeta row (trigger icon disc using a 9-key TRIGGER_EMOJIS map, trigger text, → arrow, emotion pill colored by momentDisplayInfo which uses derivedEmotionLabel for coordinate moments / legacy emotion otherwise, and a ×N badge), the localized time as a <strong>, optional note, a tag row (moment.tags only — NOT contributionTags), and Edit/Delete action buttons. Editing is INLINE (no modal): startEdit swaps the card body for a meta row + a note textarea + Save/Cancel; it lets the user edit ONLY the note (editEmotion is shown read-only as text and the trigger cannot be changed); saveEdit calls updateMoment(id, {emotion: editEmotion, note: editNote}). Delete uses window.confirm then removeMoment. Both updateMoment/removeMoment go through useSession → lib/api editMomentApi/deleteMomentApi against the same backend.

gaps.title: 2D Emotional Trajectory chart + toggle is entirely missing
gaps.description: The v1.0.14 centerpiece — the collapsible 'emotional trajectory' toggle plus the 300px valence/arousal EmotionTrajectory field — does not exist on web. Web has NO trajectory toggle, no field, no quadrant grid, no zone/corner labels, no baseline 'usual centre' ring, no recency-faded trail lines, no recency-colored/sized dots, no pulsing newest-point halo, no tappable dots, no Sparkline of valence, no count-up 'N moments · mostly <zone> · now <drift>' summary, and no legend. (web's only 'trajectory' is the unrelated sign-in-gated weekly bar trajectory in report.js.) Porting requires: a React/SVG or div-based EmotionTrajectory component, the dominantZone/baseline/drift math, a Sparkline component, the TRAJECTORY_ZONES/CORNERS constants, a pulse animation (CSS keyframes), and emotionColor parity. Tap-detail can be a popover/alert.

gaps.title: Edit-moment is a note-only inline editor, not the full bottom-sheet modal
gaps.description: Web edits ONLY the note. The emotion is displayed read-only (editEmotion as text) and the trigger cannot be changed at all; saveEdit sends {emotion: editEmotion, note}. Mobile's EditMomentModal is a bottom-sheet with a full Trigger chip grid (all TRIGGERS) AND an Emotion chip grid (all EMOTIONS) AND the note, sending {trigger, emotion, note}. Result: web users cannot re-classify a moment's trigger or emotion. Port the modal as a web dialog/bottom-sheet with trigger + emotion choosers (TRIGGERS/EMOTIONS from shared) and wire onSave to send all three fields. Backend already supports it (editMomentApi).

gaps.title: Day grouping has no relative Today/Yesterday labels and ignores locale
gaps.description: Web groupByDay always formats with toLocaleDateString('en-IN', {weekday,day,month}), so even today's section reads e.g. 'Monday, 29 Jun' instead of 'Today'/'Yesterday'. Mobile getRelativeDayLabel returns localized 'Today'/'Yesterday' for diffDays 0/1 and otherwise a hi-IN/en-IN weekday-day-month string. Port getRelativeDayLabel (relative-day logic + locale switch) into web and use it in groupByDay. Also note web hardcodes en-IN while mobile honors lang (hi-IN), so Hindi users see English day labels.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/utils/date.js (getRelativeDayLabel), mobile/screens/TimelineScreen.js (groupByDay passing t, lang)
gaps.webTarget: new web/lib/date.js or inline in web/pages/timeline.js groupByDay

gaps.title: Merge logic doesn't resolve coordinate-only moments (regression from v1.0.12)
gaps.description: Web mergeSimilarMoments compares raw last.emotion === m.emotion. For new-model moments that store only valence/arousal (no legacy emotion string), m.emotion is undefined, so two distinct coordinate-only moments with the same trigger within 30 min compare undefined===undefined and merge incorrectly; conversely identical feelings stored as coords may also mis-group. Mobile fixed this in v1.0.12 with resolveEmotion(m) (m.emotion → coordinatesToLegacy(valence,arousal) → 'neutral') used in the merge comparison. Port resolveEmotion into web and use it in mergeSimilarMoments. Web also doesn't keep the earliest timestamp nor set _grouped (minor data-shape divergence).
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/screens/TimelineScreen.js (resolveEmotion, mergeSimilarMoments)
gaps.webTarget: web/pages/timeline.js (mergeSimilarMoments + add resolveEmotion)

gaps.title: Cards show moment.tags, not adaptive contributionTags (regression from v1.0.16)
gaps.description: Web renders moment.tags?.map(...) with no overflow handling. gaps.description: Web colors cards/dots via momentDisplayInfo → colorForLabel(derivedEmotionLabel) for coordinate moments and a 5-key EMOTION_COLORS for legacy. reports.wiringNotes: Backend wiring for edit/delete is in place and correct: web/hooks/useSession.js updateMoment/removeMoment call lib/api editMomentApi/deleteMomentApi, and loadTimeline calls request('/timeline'+query) — the same /timeline, edit, and delete backend routes mobile uses (mobile services/api.js getTimeline → '/timeline', mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js updateMoment/removeMoment). One functional payload mismatch: web saveEdit sends only {emotion, note} (no trigger), while mobile sends {trigger, emotion, note}; the backend accepts trigger (mobile uses it), so web is under-sending. Identity: mobile is on device-ID identity (v1.0.9, sign-in removed) and the timeline is fully usable without an account; this timeline page itself does not gate on sign-in, so no identity regression on this surface — but verify web/hooks/useSession passes the device-ID token the same way mobile does (token is threaded through editMomentApi/deleteMomentApi). Server-provided fields the web ignores: moment.derivedLabel (preferred label on mobile) and moment.contributionTags (preferred tags on mobile) — both come back from the same backend and should be consumed on web. No analytics or subscription wiring is involved on the timeline surface. There is no timeline deep-link query param on either platform — 'View on timeline' is a post-log redirect (web index.js router.push('/timeline'), mobile EmotionSelectionScreen router.replace('/(tabs)/timeline')) and the highlight is the newest-moment-on-load animation, so deep-link parity is structurally fine; only the highlight animation fidelity differs (see gap).
reports.summary: Web timeline is materially behind mobile v1.0.17: it is missing the v1.0.14 centerpiece 2D Emotional Trajectory chart + Sparkline + toggle entirely, only lets users edit a moment's note (no trigger/emotion re-classification, no bottom-sheet modal), and lacks the v1.0.16 adaptive contributionTags and the v1.0.17 onboarding/nudge walkthrough. It also carries two correctness regressions already fixed on mobile — non-relative/locale-blind day labels and coordinate-only-moment mis-merging (v1.0.12) — plus color/label sourcing that won't match the 'Living Field' palette. Backend edit/delete wiring is correct and shares the same routes, but web under-sends the edit payload and ignores server-provided derivedLabel/contributionTags.

reports.mobileBehavior:
At v1.0.17 the Report tab is a signal-state narrative spine, not a data dashboard. WeeklyReportScreen wraps everything in a 4-segment animated SegmentedTabs control: READ (report.tabRead) | THIS WEEK (report.tabThisWeek) | PROGRESS (report.progress.tabLabel) | FOR YOU (report.tabForYou). An animated indicator slides under the active segment.

READ (default tab, the centerpiece): renders <TriggerMapView signal=deriveSignalState(report, progress) earlySignals=computeEarlySignals(recentMoments)>. deriveSignalState (utils/triggerSignal.js) collapses the whole report into ONE legible read with a resolved `state` (seeding / reflection / thread / forming / pattern / building / steady / dormant), a one-line semantic `headline`, a calibrated `barometer` (steady/shifting/building band + easing/holding/rising direction, plus a surface-vs-ground `divergence` series, never a numeric score), `connected` {friction, regulators}, `changes`, `watch` (emerging/lower-confidence), and an ordered `actions` list. TriggerMapView renders progressively: a status orb (tone color + glyph, pulsing on warning) + headline + confidence chip; then Connected patterns (friction trigger→emotion, regulators), Changes, Watch; then the EarlySignals card (gentle non-diagnostic depression/anxiety/withdrawal/body signals from the user's own moments over a 14-day window, with a supportive care note); then a RotatingAction "one thing to try" card that owns ALL the week's actions, shows one at a time, rotates daily + on a "↻ try another" tap, with helped/not_helpful feedback that persists. First/second log gets a personalized SeedExperience (SeedMap) instead of an empty shell; 0 logs / stale gets a focused StarterState. A disclaimer footer closes it.

THIS WEEK (WeekTab): a lean ordered spine — (1) "The week, written for you" GeneratedSummary (LLM narrative revealed line-by-line, tone-tinted, with a moments DeltaChip), (2) Emotional tone day-by-day trajectory scroll, (3) WeekConnected (consolidated trigger→emotion with helps/friction/neutral effect badges), (4) ShareWeekCard (share-your-week, see wiring), (5) What's working / Where to focus (top 2 each), (6) Heads-up (crash risk / false recovery / level-specific masking copy via maskingDisplay), (7) Past insights archive (collapsible, demoted to bottom), then a Timeline CTA. It deliberately DROPS the old raw frequency bars / time-of-day / current-state / vacuum / context-bleed cards.

PROGRESS (ProgressTab): trajectory arc with ProgressRing/Gauge/CountUp/Sparkline animated graphics (past→present→projected), score-trend scroll with per-week arrows, "what's changing" metric cards with AnimatedBar fills + previous markers + TrendBadge, pattern shifts (strengthening/weakening/unresolved/emerging), attributions (helped/notWorking/needsAttention), week-by-week snapshots, confidence note. Empty state shows an animated ProgressRing + filling track.

FOR YOU (PremiumTab, premium-first): an inner mode bar — Overview (free) | Move | Fuel | Perspective (premium, 🔒 when locked). Overview leads with the LLM "your direction" card (premium, or upsell/teaser for free), then free signal cards ("what's shifting" via buildSignals), premium pattern-intelligence LLM cards, free regulators/"levers" (feedback-sorted, ✓Helped badges), free behaviour snapshot metric grid, free action effectiveness (helped/not_helpful counts). Move = duration-filtered exercise list from shared MOVEMENTS, adaptive/feedback-aware, with helpful/not_helpful feedback. Fuel = diet-filtered full-day meal plan from shared NOURISHMENTS. Perspective = server LLM items. ModeCards merge server-picked items with the client catalogue, sort liked-first/disliked-last, tag emotion matches, and show a "✨ Personalised for you" vs "From our library" source tag. Premium status badge shows expiry. Everything is i18n via t() (en/hi). FTUE GuidedTooltips (insights→actions→close-loop) fire on first arrival. No sign-in anywhere: isSignedIn is hardcoded true; identity is deviceId; gate is isPremium only.
reports.webStatus: web/pages/report.js is the pre-redesign v1.0.8 app and shares almost nothing with current mobile. It has a 5-tab plain-button TabBar: Mirror | This Week | Progress | Actions | Premium (TAB_KEYS), default "mirror". MirrorTab shows current-state card, drivers, behavioral loops, deeper signals, inner-state (vacuum), context bleed, direction, what's working/where to focus. ThisWeekTab is a raw dashboard: hero "Your Week" pill header, HBar frequency bars for emotions/triggers, time-of-day bars, regulators/friction PairingChips, correlations, energy flow, stability metrics, trajectory row, micro-experiment, and a sign-in-gated LLM insight block. ProgressTab and PremiumTab are static (no animated rings/gauges/sparklines, no count-up; metrics shown as then→now text). ActionsTab lists actions with ✓Helped / ✕ Not helpful buttons. Crucially the web is still sign-in-gated: many sections render LockedSection / "Sign in to unlock" CTAs driven by isSignedIn (user && token), and the Premium tab shows "Sign in to access" when not signed in. No SegmentedTabs/animated indicator. No TriggerMapView, no signal-state read, no Read tab, no early-detection signal, no share-your-week card, no rotating action, no premium-first For You with Move/Fuel/Perspective mode bar, no SeedMap/first-log experience, no FTUE tooltips, no i18n (all English string literals).

reports.wiringNotes: Backend endpoints largely line up by NAME but identity and a couple of calls diverge. (1) IDENTITY MISMATCH (critical): web/hooks/useSession.js still treats identity as token-based — isSignedIn = Boolean(user && token), bootstrap reads a stored JWT, and all loaders (loadWeeklyReport/loadProgress/loadModes/sendActionFeedback) pass only `token`. Mobile is device-ID first: token is always null, deviceId is the identity, and every API call passes (token, deviceId) with a deviceId fallback (e.g. submitActionFeedback(actionId, response, deviceId, token), fetchProgress(token, deviceId), fetchModes(null, deviceId, lang), submitModeFeedback(mode, itemId, response, token, deviceId, source)). Web must adopt deviceId identity and thread deviceId into every report/progress/modes/feedback call, or premium/anonymous users get nothing. (2) MISSING ENDPOINTS in web/lib/api.js + useSession: createShareSnapshot (producer for share-your-week — note the CONSUMER web/pages/share/[token].js already exists), submitModeFeedback, and getWebBaseUrl. Mode feedback is therefore entirely unwired on web. (3) Web loads progress/modes lazily only when its tab is opened AND isSignedIn; mobile loads progress on mount (token||deviceId) and modes on focus only when isPremium && deviceId, and does NOT poll modes (intentional — polling caused feedback churn). (4) ANALYTICS: mobile fires trackEvent('report_screen_viewed' tier, 'mode_feedback', 'action_feedback', 'report_upgrade_tapped', 'week_shared'); web has no analyticsService calls on report. (5) SUBSCRIPTION/PREMIUM: mobile gates purely on isPremium (subscription.status active|grace_period) and drives subscribe() via handleUpgrade; web also derives isPremium the same way but additionally blocks on isSignedIn, so the upgrade path is unreachable for anonymous device-ID users. (6) The Read tab's early-detection needs raw moments — mobile calls loadTimeline() on focus and feeds computeEarlySignals; web would need the same timeline fetch wired into report. Same backend, but web is calling it with the wrong identity and is missing the share-producer and mode-feedback endpoints.

reports.mobileBehavior: PremiumScreen.js (rendered via the premium tab) is a "premium = depth" narrative sell, NOT a feature checklist. Top-to-bottom layout: (1) Hero — uppercase kicker t("premium.kicker")="Premium", a custom animated ClarityVisual SVG (13 scattered low-opacity accent dots + a rising Sparkline trend line through them, with a pulsing glow orb behind, metaphor for "signal resolving out of noise"), then title t("premium.title")="Your patterns, in focus" and subtitle "The more you log, the clearer they get." (2) Section header t("premium.outcomesHeader")="What deepens with Premium" followed by 3 outcome Cards (accent glow, staggered AppearScale delays 80+i*90), each with a title, body, and an italicized example separated by a top-bordered exampleRow with an accent dot: outcome1 "Understand the why" / weekly narrative explaining drivers / "Work stress drives frustration most on weekday evenings."; outcome2 "Catch what you can't feel yet" / drift, stability, recovery behavioral signals / "Your baseline dipped after Thursday."; outcome3 "Guidance that fits you" / adaptive Move/Fuel/Perspective modes / "Calm clusters around your morning walks - keep that." (3) An honest "Preview" hook card (FadeInView delay 360): uppercase "Preview" label, blurred preview text with a LinearGradient fade-to-bg overlay covering the bottom 30%, and an italic hint "Based on your real data." (4) A free-baseline reassurance row (lock emoji + t("premium.baselineSafe")="Your history is always saved - no account, no sign-in needed."). (5) A single CTA block: if not active, a glowing PrimaryButton labeled t("premium.unlockCta")="Unlock the full picture" (shows t("common.pleaseWait") while busy), the price label PREMIUM_PRICE_LABEL="₹149/month", and an underlined "Restore purchase" link; if active (status active or grace_period) it instead shows a green check activeCard with t("premium.activeText")="Active". Data flow: subscribe()/restoreSubscription()/subscription come from useAppSession. subscribe() calls startSubscriptionFlow(deviceId) which runs the native react-native-iap Google Play billing flow (initConnection, getSubscriptions for SKU premium_monthly with 3-retry offer-token logic, requestSubscription, then POSTs {subscriptionId, purchaseToken, deviceId} to backend /api/subscription/verify, finishTransaction), then refreshes via fetchMe(null, deviceId). Errors are mapped to localized alerts (cancelled = silent, not-found = subscriptionUnavailable, not-completed = purchaseIncomplete, else subscriptionError). restoreSubscription() calls restoreSubscriptionFlow(deviceId) (getAvailablePurchases -> verify) and alerts restored / noSubscription / restoreFailed. Identity is the deviceId only — there is no sign-in.

gaps.title: No purchase path on web — must define a web subscription strategy

Recommended approach: replace the native CTA with a web checkout (e.g. Stripe/Razorpay/Play Web checkout) whose success callback POSTs to a verify-style endpoint keyed by the web deviceId, then refreshes fetchMe; OR, if web purchase is deliberately deferred, the CTA should still render but degrade to an explicit, honest 'Premium is purchased in the Android app' state plus a deep link, while still correctly REFLECTING premium already bought on the same deviceId/account. The current page does neither cleanly (the old 'Web payments coming soon' line lives in the wrong design and there is no reflect-existing-entitlement read).

reports.wiringNotes: Backend is shared and already device-ID aware. /api/me (backend/pages/api/me.js) resolves ownerId = user?.id || req.query.deviceId and returns {user, subscription, firstAiFreeAvailable}; /api/subscription/verify accepts {subscriptionId, purchaseToken, deviceId} with ownerId = user?.id || body.deviceId, and even has a grace-period fallback when the Google API check fails. So the backend already supports an anonymous deviceId-keyed subscription read AND a verify path that web could reuse. MISMATCH: mobile hydrates subscription via fetchMe(null, deviceId) on every launch regardless of auth (useAppSession.js); web's fetchMe (web/lib/api.js line 107) is fetchMe(token) and sends NO deviceId, and useSession only calls it when a stored token exists (web/hooks/useSession.js lines 32-38). Web also still carries the removed sign-in model (loginApi/registerApi/signInWithGoogle, token in localStorage 'triggermap.web.token') even though mobile deleted sign-in in v1.0.9 — so web's isPremium derivation depends on a token that anonymous users won't have. Web does generate a stable deviceId (web/lib/api.js getDeviceId, localStorage 'triggermap.web.deviceId') and already uses it for timeline/report/export/progress/logMoment, but NOT for me/subscription. There is no web purchase path (correct — no native IAP); web must integrate a browser checkout (Stripe/Razorpay/Play web) that calls a verify-style endpoint keyed by the web deviceId, OR explicitly reflect entitlement bought elsewhere on the same identity. No premium-specific analytics events are emitted from web (mobile/backend track subscription_started/cancelled/provisional_granted); web should fire equivalents once a purchase path exists. PREMIUM_PRODUCT_ID ('premium_monthly') and PREMIUM_PRICE_LABEL ('₹149/month') come from shared/constants/premium.js so both platforms agree on SKU/price.
reports.summary: Web's premium surface is far behind: web/pages/premium.js is still the v1.0.8 static free-vs-premium feature checklist, while mobile (v1.0.9+) is a narrative 'premium = depth' screen with a custom clarity visual, outcome cards, a blurred preview hook, baseline reassurance, and a live subscribe/restore CTA driven by deviceId-keyed state. Critically, web never reads subscription state for anonymous (device-ID) users — fetchMe omits deviceId and only runs when a legacy sign-in token exists — so web users can't see or reflect premium, and there is no web purchase path at all. Reaching parity requires a full page rewrite plus rewiring useSession/fetchMe to the device-ID identity model and defining a browser checkout (web can't use native IAP) that verifies against the existing backend /api/subscription/verify endpoint.

Each toggle fires a selection() haptic and awaits the async session toggle, surfacing an Alert on error. (5) Data (icon 📂) — 'Export logs' (secondary) with hint 'Download your data as JSON', and a 'Delete all data' danger button that fires a warning() haptic then a native confirm Alert ('Delete everything?' / 'This will erase all moments, insights, and patterns. It cannot be undone.') before calling deleteAllUserData(). (6) Privacy (icon 🔒) — hint 'Your moments are saved automatically — no account needed.', Privacy policy + Terms buttons opening {webBaseUrl}/legal/* via Linking, and a Support row (qdenxp@gmail.com). (7) About (icon ℹ️) — a 'How to use TriggerMap' button that opens the GuideModal, app name, body copy, version (from Constants), developer 'QuietDen (OPC) Pvt. Ltd.', website 'qdenxp.com', footer 'Built with care.'. GuideModal mounted at bottom.

GUIDE MODAL (GuideModal.js): Full-screen slide-up Modal titled 'How TriggerMap works' / 'A guide to every module in the app' with a circular ✕ close button, and a scrollable body of 15 richly-written sections each with emoji + title + multi-sentence body: Log a moment, Triggers, Tags, Notes, The emotion scale, Your baseline, Timeline, Insights — Read & This week (free), AI narrative (Premium), For You tab, Adaptive modes (Premium), Progress over time, Notifications, Sharing, Your data & privacy. Copy is detailed and current (valence/arousal axes, baseline drift, premium-vs-free split, 'no sign-in' privacy, share-my-week 7-day link).

ONBOARDING CAROUSEL (OnboardingScreen.js, route mobile/app/onboarding.js): 6-slide horizontal paged FlatList with animated icon glow (Pulse), staggered title/body fade-ins, animated progress dots (active dot widens), a Skip link (hidden on last slide), and a Continue / 'Start logging' primary button. Slides: 🎯 Map what triggers you, ✍️ Logging takes 30 seconds, 🧭 How the scale works, 🗓 Your Timeline, 📊 Patterns & insights, ✨ Go deeper when you're ready. finish() calls completeOnboarding(), advance('framing_shown'), then router.replace('/(tabs)/log').

IN-APP GUIDED WALKTHROUGH (the v1.0.17 'full-app onboarding walkthrough'): a forward-only state machine in useOnboarding.js (OnboardingProvider, states not_started→framing_shown→first_log_done→timeline_seen→second_log_done→insights_seen→completed, persisted to AsyncStorage 'triggermap.onboarding-state', legacy-flag migration, analytics 'onboarding_advance'/'onboarding_skipped', plus per-nudge seen-tracking via markNudgeSeen/isNudgeSeen). It drives a coordinated spotlight/tooltip tour across the real tabs using SpotlightOverlay (dimmed full-screen card with emoji, message, CTA, optional Skip-guide) and GuidedTooltip/Tooltip (small auto-dismissing accent tooltips, seen-state persisted to 'triggermap.tooltip.seen.*'): Log tab shows a centered SpotlightOverlay framing card ('Each moment you log adds a data-point…' CTA 'Log your first moment', Skip guide → obSkip()), then a 'What happened? Tap the area of life it touched' trigger hint, plus a later progressive 'insights ready' nudge at 5+ moments. Emotion pad shows 'How did it affect you? Slide to where it feels right'. Timeline shows 'Each moment shows up here as it happens' then 'Over time, patterns start forming…' (advances to timeline_seen), plus a 10+ moment 'deeper patterns' nudge. Weekly report shows a 3-step chained tooltip tour: insightsExplain → actionsExplain → closeLoop (advancing insights_seen → completed).

It still has an ACCOUNT section with Status (user.email or 'Anonymous') and a Sign in / Sign out button — the exact paradigm mobile removed. planLabel is 'Premium' | 'Free' | 'Anonymous' and subscription copy branches on whether the user is signed in. Sections: Account, Subscription (✦, View plans → /premium), Notifications (🔔, three plain toggles persisted only to localStorage key 'tm_notification_prefs' — no OS permission handling, no backend, no haptics), Data (📂, Export logs + Delete all data via window.confirm/alert), Privacy (🔒, policy/terms links + support), About (ℹ️, version hard-coded 'v1.1.0', no user-guide button). There is NO Language selector, NO guide modal, NO 'How to use TriggerMap' entry, NO accent glow/stagger animations, and copy/structure diverges from mobile. The web app has ZERO onboarding: no /onboarding route, no carousel, no OnboardingProvider, no useOnboarding, no SpotlightOverlay/GuidedTooltip/Tooltip components, and no in-app guided tour anywhere. reports.wiringNotes: Identity mismatch is the deepest issue: web/hooks/useSession.js is still a token/email/Google auth model (signInWithEmail, registerWithEmail, signInWithGoogle, signOut, getStoredToken, isSignedIn) and every load*/save*/export/delete call is gated on `token`. Mobile abandoned sign-in for device-ID identity (deviceId IS the identity; premium = depth). Web does have getDeviceId() in lib/api.js but only passes it as a hint during login, and deleteAllUserData() is a no-op when there is no token — so an anonymous web user cannot actually delete their data. Notifications on web are stored ONLY in localStorage ('tm_notification_prefs') and are never sent to the backend or registered as push subscriptions, whereas mobile's toggleReflection/toggleReminder/toggleNudges (in useAppSession) schedule/cancel real notifications and persist real prefs; web therefore has no working notification delivery (no PWA Push/web-push registration). Onboarding state machine + nudge/tooltip seen-flags are AsyncStorage-backed on mobile and have no localStorage equivalent on web, so a ported tour has no persistence layer yet. Analytics events ('onboarding_advance', 'onboarding_skipped') emitted by mobile's useOnboarding have no web counterpart. Language ('en'/'hi') is a real mobile setting via useLanguage/LanguageContext with hi.json; web has no i18n layer at all. Guide/onboarding copy lives in mobile/i18n/en.json (onboarding.*, ftue.*, nudge.*) and in GuideModal's hardcoded SECTIONS — none of this exists in web. Web 'About' version is hard-coded ('v1.1.0') rather than read from a shared version source.
