None of this exists on web. A ported tour needs equivalent localStorage-backed persistence so steps/nudges/tooltips show exactly once and survive reloads, plus analytics event parity. Without it the tour would re-trigger on every visit.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/hooks/useOnboarding.js (STORAGE_KEY, NUDGE_PREFIX, AsyncStorage.setItem, trackEvent), mobile/components/Tooltip.js (PREFIX 'triggermap.tooltip.seen.'), mobile/services/analyticsService.js
gaps.webTarget: web/hooks/useOnboarding.js (localStorage), web/components/Tooltip.js (localStorage), web analytics util

reports.summary: For this surface the web app is dramatically behind. Two of the three audited features — the entire in-app onboarding walkthrough (carousel + spotlight/tooltip guided tour + state machine) and the 15-section Guide modal — simply do not exist on web, and the web Settings page is structurally stale: it still ships the sign-in/Account paradigm mobile deleted in v1.0.9, has no Language selector, no guide entry, and notification toggles that only write to localStorage. Bringing this surface to parity is a large effort dominated by building the onboarding/tour infrastructure from scratch and reworking web identity off the token/auth model onto device-ID.

reports.mobileBehavior:
As of v1.0.17 mobile has NO sign-in, NO account, NO auth token. Identity = a single device-scoped UUID. Flow:

IDENTITY GENERATION (deviceService.js): getOrCreateDeviceId() reads AsyncStorage key "triggermap.device-id"; if absent, on native it falls back to SecureStore (key DEVICE_ID_KEY), else generates Crypto.randomUUID(), persists to AsyncStorage and (native) SecureStore. There is no email/password/Google anywhere. SecureStore session token ("triggermap.session-token") is now READ-ONLY legacy: never written, read once during recovery then cleared (getSessionToken/clearSessionToken). Recovery one-shot guard is AsyncStorage "triggermap.recovery-done" (getRecoveryDone/setRecoveryDone).

SESSION BOOTSTRAP (useAppSession.js SessionProvider): on mount it Promise.all's getOrCreateDeviceId + onboarding/reminder/reflection/nudge prefs, then setDeviceId(storedDeviceId). State exposes deviceId plus DELIBERATELY-STABLE-NULLS token:null and user:null (comment: 'There is no account or token anymore' — kept null so anonymous code paths work unchanged). Then it: (1) runs ONE-TIME RECOVERY — if !recoveryDone, reads legacy SecureStore token and calls recover(deviceId, legacyToken); only on result.ok does it setRecoveryDone(true) and clearSessionToken (else retries next launch); (2) registerDevice(deviceId) fire-and-forget so install shows in ops console; (3) fetchMe(null, deviceId) to hydrate subscription + firstAiFreeAvailable; (4) drains pending offline sync queue; (5) registers Expo push token by deviceId; (6) saveNotificationPrefs by deviceId. A 10s safety timer force-unblocks ready. ensureDeviceIdentity() lazily guarantees a deviceId before any write.

DATA FLOW: offline-first. saveMoment writes localStore first (instant Timeline), setLastLoggedAt, then fire-and-forget logMoment({deviceId,...}) with a retry queue (queuePendingSync) on failure. loadTimeline merges backend (fetchTimeline(deviceId) authoritative, holds recovered history) with localStore, server-wins on id, offline-falls-back to local. loadWeeklyReport uses fetchWeeklyReport(deviceId) authoritative, buildLocalReport offline fallback. updateMoment/removeMoment hit both local and editMoment/deleteMomentApi with deviceId. subscribe()/restoreSubscription() use deviceId; deleteAllUserData() calls deleteAllData(null, deviceId)+clearLocalMoments. EVERY api call passes token=null and a deviceId.

api.js: all endpoints take (payload, token, ...) but with token always null they append ?deviceId= (timeline/weeklyReport/me/export/progress/modes/deleteData/deleteMoment) or put deviceId in the body (logMoment/editMoment/recover/registerDevice/push-token/notification-prefs/share). recover(deviceId, token) POSTs /recover with body {deviceId} and the legacy token as Bearer.
reports.webStatus: Web is fully account-centric, frozen at v1.0.8. web/pages/login.js renders a full Google Sign-In (hardcoded GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID 773449945543-...) + email/password register/login form, with a 'Continue without signing in' escape hatch. web/hooks/useSession.js exposes signInWithEmail/registerWithEmail/signInWithGoogle/signOut, isSignedIn=Boolean(user&&token), and bootstraps ONLY when a stored JWT exists (getStoredToken from localStorage 'triggermap.web.token'); for anonymous users it fetches NOTHING (no /me, no subscription, no firstAiFree). web/lib/api.js HAS a getDeviceId() (localStorage 'triggermap.web.deviceId', crypto.randomUUID) and appends ?deviceId= on GET reads (timeline/weeklyReport/export/progress) and in logMoment/actionFeedback bodies — so anonymous READS work — but: fetchMe(token) takes NO deviceId; editMomentApi/deleteMomentApi send NO deviceId; deleteAllDataApi(token) sends NO deviceId; loginApi/registerApi still exist and POST /login,/register. web/components/Layout.js shows a 'Sign in' nav link + 'Signed in as {email}' / device-ID hint. web/pages/settings.js shows Account status email/Anonymous, Sign out/Sign in buttons routing to /login. web/pages/report.js gates Patterns/Premium/Progress/AI-insight tabs behind isSignedIn with 'Sign in to unlock' CTAs routing to /login. NO recover() call, NO registerDevice() call, NO firstAiFreeAvailable, NO offline localStore/merge, NO retry queue.

gaps.title: Delete web sign-in entirely (login page, Google SDK, email/password)
gaps.description: Mobile removed sign-in completely in v1.0.9 (deleted LoginScreen.js, app/login.js, withGoogleSignIn.js). Web still ships web/pages/login.js (Google GSI button with hardcoded client id, email/password register+login form) and loads the Google SDK script in _app.js. To reach parity: delete web/pages/login.js; remove the <Script src='accounts.google.com/gsi/client'> from web/pages/_app.js; remove the GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID constant. There is no sign-in surface in mobile at all — deviceId is the only identity.
gaps.mobileSource: v1.0.9 commit 9ec385d: deleted mobile/screens/LoginScreen.js, mobile/app/login.js, mobile/plugins/withGoogleSignIn.js; mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js (token:null,user:null comment block)
gaps.webTarget: DELETE web/pages/login.js; EDIT web/pages/_app.js (drop Google Script); EDIT web/hooks/useSession.js

gaps.title: Rewrite useSession to device-ID identity (drop token/user/JWT, mirror useAppSession)
gaps.description: web/hooks/useSession.js is built around a JWT (token state, getStoredToken/setStoredToken localStorage 'triggermap.web.token', isSignedIn) and exposes signInWithEmail/registerWithEmail/signInWithGoogle/signOut. Mobile's SessionProvider exposes deviceId as the single owner id and keeps token:null/user:null as stable nulls. Port: on bootstrap call getDeviceId() unconditionally (no token gate) and fetchMe(null, deviceId) to hydrate subscription + add firstAiFreeAvailable state (currently absent on web). Remove signIn*/signOut/isSignedIn. Keep token always null so existing call sites that thread token keep working, exactly as mobile did. Add ensureDeviceIdentity() lazy getter. Expose deviceId.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js (SessionProvider: deviceId state, fetchMe(null, storedDeviceId), firstAiFreeAvailable, ensureDeviceIdentity, token:null/user:null)
gaps.webTarget: web/hooks/useSession.js (full rewrite)

gaps.title: fetchMe never passes deviceId — anonymous web users never hydrate subscription / firstAiFree
gaps.description: Backend /api/me (backend/pages/api/me.js) derives ownerId = user?.id || req.query.deviceId and 400s 'MISSING_OWNER' if neither is present. web/lib/api.js fetchMe(token) sends NO deviceId, and useSession only calls it when a token exists. So an anonymous web user (the only kind post-migration) NEVER loads subscription or firstAiFreeAvailable — premium gating silently breaks. Mobile calls fetchMe(null, deviceId) with ?deviceId=. Fix: change web fetchMe to fetchMe(token, deviceId) appending ?deviceId= when token-less (mirror mobile api.js fetchMe), and call it on every bootstrap.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js fetchMe(token, deviceId); mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js bootstrap fetchMe(null, storedDeviceId); backend/pages/api/me.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js (fetchMe signature), web/hooks/useSession.js (bootstrap)

gaps.title: No one-time data-recovery call on web (signed-in web users' data stays stranded)
gaps.description: v1.0.10 added the recovery story: mobile calls recover(deviceId, legacyToken) once on first launch to copy account data keyed by old userId onto deviceId; backend POST /api/recover -> recoverDeviceIfNeeded(deviceId, token) derives the account from the leftover Bearer token OR the device->account link, copies non-destructively, and marks recovered:{deviceId}. Web has NO recover() function and never calls it. Web users who previously signed in (the web app had Google/email login!) have their data stranded under their userId. Web must: (a) read its leftover JWT from localStorage 'triggermap.web.token' ONCE, (b) call a new recover(deviceId, token) that POSTs /api/recover {deviceId} with Bearer token, (c) on result.ok set a 'triggermap.web.recovery-done' flag and clear the old token, else retry next load. Backend self-heals on /timeline,/weeklyReport,/progress reads too, but token-driven recover is needed for web users whose deviceId was never linked (web previously linked deviceId->user via loginApi({deviceId})).
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js recover(deviceId, token); mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js recovery block (getRecoveryDone/getSessionToken/recover/setRecoveryDone/clearSessionToken); mobile/services/deviceService.js RECOVERY_KEY/SESSION_TOKEN_KEY; backend/pages/api/recover.js + backend/services/dataMigration.js recoverDeviceIfNeeded
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js (add recover), web/hooks/useSession.js (bootstrap recovery block + recovery-done flag + read/clear legacy token)

gaps.title: No registerDevice call on web (installs invisible to ops console)
gaps.description: Mobile fire-and-forgets registerDevice(deviceId) at bootstrap so every install is discoverable in the ops console and counted as an anonymous owner, independent of logging/push. Backend POST /api/register-device {deviceId} (SADD owners + HSETNX user + touchDailyActive). Web has no equivalent, so web installs/devices are never registered as owners or daily-active. Add registerDevice(deviceId) to web/lib/api.js and call it (fire-and-forget) in useSession bootstrap.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js registerDevice(deviceId); mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js registerDevice(storedDeviceId).catch; backend/pages/api/register-device.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js (add registerDevice), web/hooks/useSession.js (bootstrap)

gaps.title: deleteAllUserData is a no-op for anonymous web users; deleteAllDataApi omits deviceId
gaps.description: web/hooks/useSession.js deleteAllUserData() is wrapped in `if (token) {...}` so an anonymous user (everyone now) can never delete their data. And web/lib/api.js deleteAllDataApi(token) sends no deviceId, while backend deleteData.js needs ownerId = user?.id || req.query.deviceId. Mobile does deleteAllData(null, deviceId) and clears localStore. Fix: deleteAllDataApi(token, deviceId) appending ?deviceId= when token-less; remove the if(token) guard so anonymous delete works; also clear local web state.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js deleteAllData(token, deviceId); mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js deleteAllUserData(); backend/pages/api/deleteData.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js (deleteAllDataApi), web/hooks/useSession.js (deleteAllUserData)

gaps.title: editMoment / deleteMoment on web omit deviceId — anonymous edits/deletes 400 or no-op
gaps.description: backend/pages/api/moment/[id].js derives ownerId = user?.id || req.body?.deviceId (PUT) || req.query.deviceId (DELETE) and 400s without it. web/lib/api.js editMomentApi/deleteMomentApi send NO deviceId, so anonymous web edits/deletes fail (no token, no deviceId => MISSING_OWNER). Mobile sends editMoment(id,{...updates, deviceId}) and deleteMomentApi(id, null, deviceId) (?deviceId=). Fix web editMomentApi to merge deviceId into the body and deleteMomentApi to append ?deviceId= when token-less.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js editMoment/deleteMomentApi; mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js updateMoment/removeMoment; backend/pages/api/moment/[id].js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js (editMomentApi, deleteMomentApi)

gaps.title: Remove all sign-in UI/CTAs from Layout, Settings, and Report
gaps.description: Mobile de-gated Settings + Weekly Report in v1.0.9 (no sign-in CTAs, no dead /login links — premium=depth, all early-detection data is client-available). Web still shows: Layout.js nav 'Sign in' link + 'Signed in as {email}' hint; settings.js Account section with email/Anonymous status + Sign out/Sign in buttons -> /login; report.js gating Patterns/Premium/Progress/AI tabs behind isSignedIn with 'Sign in to unlock' buttons -> /login (lines ~266,513,517,622-624,725-733,1130-1136,1355-1365,1394-1398). These must be replaced: remove sign-in nav/CTAs; gate premium features on isPremium (subscription status) NOT isSignedIn; show 'Anonymous' identity copy that matches mobile. Note backend weeklyReport premium gating was already moved off isAuthenticated to ownerId in v1.0.9.
gaps.mobileSource: v1.0.9 commit 9ec385d: mobile/screens/SettingsScreen.js, mobile/screens/WeeklyReportScreen.js, mobile/screens/PremiumScreen.js (de-gated, premium-by-subscription)
gaps.webTarget: web/components/Layout.js, web/pages/settings.js, web/pages/report.js

gaps.title: Premium/report gating uses isSignedIn instead of subscription status
gaps.description: report.js threads isSignedIn (Boolean(user&&token)) to gate Patterns/Premium/Progress/AI-insight, and only fetches progress/modes when isSignedIn. Post-migration NOBODY is signed in, so every web user would see locked content forever even though the backend serves these by deviceId and premium is keyed by subscription on ownerId. Replace the isSignedIn gate with: always allow client-available early-detection data (per memory: all early-detection data is client-available, only the LLM narrative is premium), and gate the AI narrative on isPremium = subscription.status active/grace_period. Fetch progress/modes by deviceId unconditionally.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js (subscription/firstAiFreeAvailable, no isSignedIn); shared redesign per Trigger Map redesign memory note
gaps.webTarget: web/pages/report.js

gaps.title: Web deviceId is not persisted to a durable store and uses a different key namespace than recovery expects
gaps.description: web/lib/api.js getDeviceId() persists to localStorage 'triggermap.web.deviceId' with a 'web-'+Date.now() fallback when crypto.randomUUID is unavailable. Mobile uses 'triggermap.device-id' (UUID, native SecureStore-backed). This is acceptable for web (the deviceService.js comment explicitly says web persists deviceId via localStorage and that's sufficient), but note: (a) the web deviceId namespace 'triggermap.web.deviceId' differs from mobile, which is fine since they are separate installs; (b) the non-UUID fallback 'web-'+Date.now() could collide and is < the backend register-device min length check is 8 (ok) — but recovery linkage relies on the SAME deviceId that was sent in the old loginApi({deviceId}) calls, so getDeviceId must keep returning the SAME stored value, which it does. No change strictly required, but consider a UUID-only fallback for robustness.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/deviceService.js getOrCreateDeviceId (web branch comment); mobile/services/api.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js (getDeviceId, optional)

gaps.title: No offline-first localStore / merge / retry queue on web (functional parity, not strictly identity)
gaps.description: Identity-adjacent: mobile's session does offline-first writes (saveLocalMoment, queuePendingSync, merge server+local in loadTimeline, buildLocalReport fallback) keyed by deviceId. Web's useSession saveMoment/loadTimeline are thin pass-throughs to the API with no local cache, no retry queue, no offline merge. Full parity for the data layer would port mobile/services/localStore.js patterns, but this is a data-layer gap rather than pure identity; flagging because the device-ID session is the owner of these flows.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/localStore.js; mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js saveMoment/loadTimeline/loadWeeklyReport
gaps.webTarget: web/hooks/useSession.js, new web/lib/localStore.js

reports.wiringNotes:
Backend is ALREADY fully device-ID ready — no backend changes needed for identity. Every consumed endpoint derives ownerId = (token ? validateSession(token).id : null) || req.query.deviceId || req.body.deviceId and 400s MISSING_OWNER only if BOTH are absent: confirmed in backend/pages/api/me.js, deleteData.js, moment/[id].js, and (from mobile api.js) timeline/weeklyReport/export/progress/modes/notification-prefs all take ?deviceId=. login.js/register.js still exist server-side (kept 'fully backward-compatible' per v1.0.9 commit) but web should stop calling them.

IDENTITY: mobile identity = a generated UUID in AsyncStorage key 'triggermap.device-id' (SecureStore-backed on native), sent as a deviceId QUERY PARAM on GETs and a deviceId BODY FIELD on writes — it is NOT an HTTP header and NOT a Bearer token. Both mobile and web send Authorization: Bearer only when a token exists (now never on mobile). Web already has getDeviceId() (localStorage 'triggermap.web.deviceId') and already threads it on reads + logMoment; the gaps are fetchMe/editMoment/deleteMoment/deleteData missing it, plus no recover/registerDevice and a token-gated bootstrap.

RECOVERY/IDENTITY-LINK: device->account link lives in redis deviceUser:{deviceId}->userId, set by authService.linkDeviceToUser whenever a device signed in (web's old loginApi/registerApi passed deviceId, so web devices that ever logged in ARE linked). recoverDeviceIfNeeded(deviceId, token) prefers that link, trusts a matching Bearer token, heals a missing link, copies non-destructively (dataMigration.migrateOwnerData), and marks recovered:{deviceId}. Backend ALSO self-heals on /timeline,/weeklyReport,/progress reads, so even without an explicit web recover() call, link-present web users auto-recover on first read; but web users whose link is missing need the token-driven recover() (which web does not yet call). SUBSCRIPTION/PREMIUM is keyed by ownerId (subscription:{ownerId}) and firstAiFree by first_ai_claimed:{ownerId} — web currently never hydrates either for anonymous users.

ANALYTICS/PUSH: mobile registers push token + notification prefs + register-device by deviceId; web has none of these (web stores notification prefs only in localStorage 'tm_notification_prefs', never synced to backend) — out of strict identity scope but worth noting for the install-visibility gap.
reports.summary: Web is a full version behind on identity: it still runs the entire removed sign-in stack (login page, Google GSI, email/password, JWT-based useSession, isSignedIn gating) while mobile v1.0.17 has zero sign-in and treats a generated deviceId as the sole identity. The backend is already fully device-ID compatible, so the work is web-side only: delete login, rewrite useSession to bootstrap on deviceId + fetchMe(deviceId)+recover()+registerDevice(), thread deviceId through fetchMe/editMoment/deleteMoment/deleteData, and replace every isSignedIn gate with subscription-based premium gating. Critically, anonymous web users today never hydrate subscription/firstAiFree and previously-signed-in web users' data stays stranded because web makes no recover() call.

reports.surface: Visual design system & atmosphere (backgrounds, palette, motion, tab bar, fonts, cards) — "The Living Field" (v1.0.13) + clean icons/promoted tabs (v1.0.14) + leaner visual components (v1.0.15–1.0.17)
reports.mobileBehavior: Every mobile screen is wrapped in ScreenShell, which renders a deep-space LinearGradient base (#070c18 → #05080f → #03050b) with a full-bleed living AtmosphericField behind all content. AtmosphericField draws 4 large soft-edged SVG radial-gradient blobs that continuously drift, breathe (opacity 0.62→1.0), rotate and scale on the Reanimated native thread (durations 26–39s, staggered delays). Blob hues come from an AURORA triad keyed to the user's current dominant emotion (calm/neutral/anxious/frustrated/energized), each emotion mapping to a [primary, secondary, deep] color set. A radial vignette (cx 50%/34%, deepens edges/bottom to #03050b @ 0.82) sits on top for depth. Field intensity drops to 0.6 before any history exists. The dominant emotion + intensity are supplied globally by the useEmotionalState provider (weighted-recency average of the last 48h of moments, with a legacy coordinate fallback so new valence/arousal moments still tint the field). The tab bar (v1.0.14) is a bottom Expo-router Tabs bar with 5 promoted tabs in order Log/Timeline/Insights/Premium/Settings using clean Ionicons outline glyphs (flash-outline, time-outline, sparkles-outline, diamond-outline, settings-outline), active tint = the live emotion color, inactive = palette.muted, 11px 600-weight labels, palette.background bg with a hairline glassBorder top border, height accounting for safe-area inset; tab press fires a haptic. Motion system (motion.js + theme.motion tokens): FadeInView (fade+rise, directional), Stagger (sequential child reveal, 70ms step), PressableScale (spring 0.96 press + selection haptic), Pulse (infinite breathing scale+opacity), Shimmer (skeleton sweep), AppearScale (spring pop-in), useCountUp/CountUpText (animated number count-up). graphics.js adds animated SVG data-viz: ProgressRing, 270° Gauge, Catmull-Rom Sparkline with draw-on, MiniBars, AnimatedBar. Cards (Card.js) are glass surfaces: rounded radius.lg, palette.card bg, glassBorder, a diagonal LinearGradient sheen, a top hairline highlight, optional accent left-edge + glow shadow, and an entrance FadeInView. PrimaryButton: 54px min-height, radius.md, accentStrong fill with cyan glow shadow, plus secondary/danger/outline variants and a pressed scale 0.96. Palette is a cyan-forward dark theme (accent #56d0e0, success #5ee6a0, warning #ffb347, danger #ff6b7a, purple #a78bfa, text #edf2fa). EmotionGarden, MoodWeather, StreakOrb, and the Signal radial band-dial (v1.0.14 Insights centerpiece) are all richly animated (per-emotion living motion, centroid 2D valence/arousal mini-plane, paused/lapsed streak state, animated count, sweeping dial marker with pulse). Mobile uses the platform system font (San Francisco / Roboto), tracked tight on hero/title type.
reports.webStatus: Web is at its v1.0.8 state. Layout.js renders a desktop-style "shell" (max-width 860px centered) with a hero header (eyebrow + big h1 + lede paragraph + backend-status badge), a horizontal scrolling PILL nav of TEXT LINKS (Log moment / Timeline / Weekly report / Settings / Sign in) — not an icon tab bar — and a static background. The background atmosphere is three fixed, blurred (filter blur 100px) radial glow blobs (shellGlowOne/Two/Three) defined in globals.css that breathe opacity/scale on an 8s loop but NEVER change color with emotion and do not drift. body background is a fixed multi-radial + linear gradient (#080e1a → #060a12 → #040710). There is NO AtmosphericField equivalent and NO global useEmotionalState provider; instead each page (index/report/timeline) drops a single per-page `.stateGlow` — one static-color 400px radial blob pinned top-center, color chosen once from that page's dominant emotion, pulsing opacity only. globals.css has CSS-keyframe equivalents of some motion (sceneIn, tileStagger, chipPulse, bloomPop, orbPulse, breatheDot, shimmer) and full styling for cards (.card radius 22px), buttons, sliders, report tabs (a sticky in-page .reportTabBar of text buttons), and the garden/weather/streak components. Web EmotionGarden/MoodWeather/StreakOrb are the simplified v1.0.8 versions. Font is "Trebuchet MS", "Avenir Next", system-ui — a deliberately different typeface from mobile.

gaps.title: No living emotion-driven AtmosphericField — the single biggest visual gap
gaps.description: Web has no equivalent of the v1.0.13 'Living Field'. Mobile renders 4 drifting/breathing/rotating/scaling SVG radial-gradient aurora blobs whose hues come from a per-emotion AURORA triad, plus a radial vignette, behind every screen at 60fps. Web only has 3 fixed, fixed-color blurred glow blobs (shellGlow*) that breathe opacity but never drift or change color, plus an optional single static per-page stateGlob. Need to build a web AtmosphericField: a fixed full-viewport layer with ~4 large CSS radial-gradient (or blurred) blobs animated via CSS @keyframes for drift+scale+opacity (translate/scale transforms), hue triad keyed to dominant emotion, staggered animation-delays, plus a radial-gradient vignette overlay. Port the AURORA map {calm/neutral/anxious/frustrated/energized → [primary,secondary,deep]} and the deep-space base gradient (#070c18→#05080f→#03050b) and intensity-0.6 cold-start behavior.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/components/AtmosphericField.js (AURORA map, Blob, blobs config, vignette), mobile/components/ScreenShell.js (gradientColors, fieldIntensity)
gaps.webTarget: new web/components/AtmosphericField.js + new keyframes/classes in web/styles/globals.css; mount it in web/components/Layout.js (replacing static shellGlow blobs)

gaps.title: No global emotional-state provider driving the atmosphere
gaps.description: Mobile has useEmotionalState (a React context provider) that computes dominantEmotion, dominantTrigger, emotionalTrend, emotionColor, glow colors and momentCount from a weighted-recency average of the last 48h of moments (with a coordinate→legacy fallback), refreshes on mount + app-foreground, and feeds the atmosphere AND the tab bar active tint globally. Web has no such provider; it recomputes a dominant emotion ad-hoc inside individual pages (index.js labelColor, report.js stateColor, timeline.js dominantEmotion) purely to color one static stateGlow blob. Port a useEmotionalState hook/provider (mount in web/pages/_app.js) with the same computeDominantEmotion/Trigger/Trend logic and EMOTION_PALETTE, so the new AtmosphericField, nav, and accents all react consistently to one source of truth.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/hooks/useEmotionalState.js (EmotionalStateProvider, computeDominantEmotion/Trigger/Trend, EMOTION_PALETTE, SCORE, momentEmotion)
gaps.webTarget: new web/hooks/useEmotionalState.js; wrap web/pages/_app.js; consume in Layout.js + AtmosphericField

gaps.title: Tab bar: web uses text-link pill nav, not the v1.0.14 promoted icon tab bar
gaps.description: Mobile v1.0.14 promoted tabs to a bottom 5-item icon tab bar: Log/Timeline/Insights/Premium/Settings with clean Ionicons outline glyphs (flash-outline, time-outline, sparkles-outline, diamond-outline, settings-outline), active tint = live emotion color, inactive = muted, 11px labels, haptic on press. Web Layout.js still renders the v1.0.8 horizontal text-link pill nav (links: 'Log moment','Timeline','Weekly report','Settings','Sign in') with no icons, wrong labels ('Weekly report' vs 'Insights', no 'Premium'), wrong order, and a stale 'Sign in' link (sign-in was removed at v1.0.9). Rebuild as an icon tab bar (SVG icons matching the Ionicons outline set), correct 5-item set/order/labels, active tint driven by emotion color, remove the Sign-in link. On mobile-width it should read as a bottom tab bar; the report page's separate in-page .reportTabBar (mirror/week/progress/actions/premium) is unrelated and should stay.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/navigation/tabOptions.js (createTabOptions, icons, tints, label style), mobile/app/(tabs)/_layout.js (tab set/order/labels, emotionColor active tint, tap haptic)
gaps.webTarget: web/components/Layout.js (.nav block), web/styles/globals.css (.nav, .nav a) — needs new bottom-tab-bar + icon styles

gaps.description: Mobile has a dedicated Premium tab (diamond-outline icon) in the primary tab bar; a web/pages/premium.js page exists but is NOT linked from Layout.js nav, so the Premium surface is unreachable from navigation. Add Premium to the web nav/tab bar in the correct position (4th, between Insights and Settings).

gaps.title: Font mismatch: web 'Trebuchet MS' vs mobile system font
gaps.description: Mobile uses the platform system font (San Francisco on iOS / Roboto on Android) with the theme.type scale (hero 34/-0.5, title 26/-0.3, kicker 11/1.4 uppercase). Web globals.css sets font-family to 'Trebuchet MS','Avenir Next',system-ui — a visibly different, rounder typeface that breaks parity, plus uses fluid clamp() hero sizing rather than the fixed type scale. Switch web base font to a system-ui / Roboto-leaning stack (or bundle Inter as a neutral cross-platform match) and align the type scale + letter-spacing tokens to theme.type.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/utils/theme.js (type scale), RN default fontFamily

gaps.title: EmotionGarden: web is the simplified v1.0.8 version — missing centroid plane, per-emotion living motion, field-color blooms
gaps.description: Mobile EmotionGarden groups today's moments into up-to-8 blooms sized by frequency, each with a per-emotion living animation (float/breathe/jitter/shake/pulse via EMOTION_ANIM+ANIM_CONFIGS), a spring growth entrance, tap-to-expand detail (count/top trigger/peak period) with haptic, a highlight pulse, an intensity-proportional glow, a mood-tinted warmth gradient background, and a 36px centroid 2D valence/arousal mini-plane with a colored dot ('today's center'). Web EmotionGarden only renders static bloomPop chips (icon+label) with a single CSS animation-delay, a fixed seed/bloom emoji map (5 legacy emotions), no centroid plane, no per-emotion motion, no expand/detail, no warmth tint, and uses EMOTION_STYLES.bg/border rather than the continuous emotionColor(valence,arousal). Port the grouping/centroid math and per-emotion motion (CSS variants or JS), the centroid mini-plane, expand-on-tap, and the field-color mapping.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/components/EmotionGarden.js (blooms/centroid useMemo, BloomItem per-emotion motion, centroid mini-plane), mobile/utils/emotionModel.js (emotionColor)
gaps.webTarget: web/components/EmotionGarden.js, web/styles/globals.css (.gardenWrap/.bloomSlot/centroid styles), web/lib/emotionModel (new — emotionColor port)

gaps.title: StreakOrb: web missing paused/lapsed state and continuous pulse tiers
gaps.description: Mobile StreakOrb returns null only for a genuinely new user; a returning user whose streak lapsed gets a 'paused' state (🌙 + best-streak acknowledgement) instead of silently vanishing, computes a best-streak, and pulses (scale+opacity, streak>=2) with 4 tiers (spark/building/strong/legendary). Web StreakOrb returns null whenever streak<1 (no paused state, no computeBestStreak), and while the glow CSS pulses (orbPulse) there is no streak>=2 gating. Port the paused state, computeBestStreak, and tier/pulse parity.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/components/StreakOrb.js (paused branch, computeBestStreak, TIER_MAP, pulse gating)
gaps.webTarget: web/components/StreakOrb.js, web/styles/globals.css (.streakOrb* + paused styles)

gaps.title: MoodWeather: web copy/desc hardcoded in English; lacks i18n + uses static score mapping
gaps.description: Mobile MoodWeather pulls label/desc from i18n (t('weather.*')) and uses momentEmotion with coordinate fallback for variance/electric checks; web MoodWeather hardcodes English label/desc strings in WEATHER_MAP and buckets only by legacy m.emotion (m.emotion||'neutral') for the mixed/electric checks, so new valence/arousal-only moments are mis-bucketed. The visual (shimmer sweep, breathe dot) is otherwise close. Align bucketing to use a coordinate fallback and route copy through the web i18n once that exists.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/components/MoodWeather.js (momentEmotion fallback, t() copy)
gaps.webTarget: web/components/MoodWeather.js

gaps.title: Signal radial band-dial (v1.0.14 Insights centerpiece) has no web equivalent
gaps.description: The v1.0.14 Insights overhaul centers on Signal.js: a 270° SVG band dial (Steady/Shifting/Building zones) with a marker that sweeps to live pressure on mount and pulses when 'Building', a headline, drivers, confidence chip, and a surface-vs-underneath divergence chart (Catmull-Rom smoothed dual-line with gap fill). Web report.js uses flat CSS bars/badges (progressArc, trendBadge, stateOfMindCard) with none of this animated radial-dial atmosphere. Build an SVG band-dial component for web with the same zone boundaries (0–0.4/0.4–0.62/0.62–1), sweep animation, pulse, and divergence chart.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/components/triggermap/Signal.js (BandDial, ZONES, arcPath, DivergenceChart, smoothPath), mobile/components/graphics.js (Gauge/Sparkline patterns)

gaps.title: Card sheen + accent edge + top hairline highlight not fully replicated
gaps.description: Mobile Card.js layers a diagonal LinearGradient sheen (start 0.1,0 → 0.9,1, locations [0,0.45,1]), a top hairline highlight (rgba(255,255,255,0.14) inset between radius gaps), an optional 3px accent left-edge, an accent glow shadow, and an entrance FadeInView. Web .card uses a simple vertical linear-gradient (white 0.06→0.03) with a flat border and box-shadow — no diagonal sheen, no top hairline, no accent left-edge primitive, no per-card entrance fade (only .sceneIn on some stacks). Add a reusable web Card with the diagonal sheen pseudo-element, top hairline, accent edge, and entrance animation to match.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/components/Card.js (sheen LinearGradient, topHighlight, edge, glow), mobile/utils/theme.js (shadow.glow)
gaps.webTarget: web/styles/globals.css (.card and a new accent/sheen variant)

gaps.title: Continuous emotionColor(valence,arousal) mapping not ported to web
gaps.description: Mobile emotionModel.emotionColor maps continuous valence/arousal to one of ~7 colors (energized cyan, tense red, alert amber, calm/content green, low purple, settled blue-grey, neutral muted) and is used to tint blooms, centroid dots, and field accents. Web designSystem.js has DERIVED_LABEL_COLORS/REGION_COLORS/colorForLabel but no continuous emotionColor function, so web components fall back to discrete EMOTION_STYLES bg/border. Port emotionColor + FIELD_GRADIENT so web visuals tint by continuous coordinates like mobile.

gaps.title: PrimaryButton variants & glow not matched (radius shape mismatch)

gaps.title: Motion token + curve drift between platforms
gaps.description: Mobile centralizes motion in theme.motion (durations instant120/fast220/base360/slow560/count900/breath4000, stagger70, spring presets, press0.96) and uses Easing.out(cubic)/inOut(ease). Web uses ad-hoc CSS durations and cubic-bezier(0.25,1,0.5,1) for sceneIn (0.45–0.5s), 0.4s bloomPop, 1.8s orbPulse, 8s shellGlow, 6s stateGlow, 4s shimmer — close in spirit but not derived from shared tokens, and some (FadeInView directional rise, AppearScale spring pop, count-up) have no web analog beyond sceneIn. gaps.title: Hero/eyebrow header is a web-only desktop affordance not present on mobile

gaps.title: Loading/skeleton state parity (ScreenShell loader)
gaps.description: Mobile ScreenShell has a built-in loading state: a glowing accent loader orb (ActivityIndicator in accentSoft circle), title/body copy, placeholder skeleton cards (large + two small) with cardGlow bg and glassBorder, a 3s timeout message, and a retry button. reports.wiringNotes: The design-system/atmosphere layer is presentation-only, so there are no NEW backend endpoints required for parity — both apps already read moments from the same backend (web/lib/api.js fetchHealth + timeline; mobile via useAppSession/loadTimeline). The relevant wiring gap is identity-coupling, not the design system: mobile drives the entire atmosphere from useEmotionalState, which loads the timeline (keyed by deviceId per MEMORY device-id-identity) and computes dominantEmotion/color/intensity globally; web has no such provider and recomputes a per-page dominant emotion locally for a single stateGlow blob. To wire the new web AtmosphericField correctly it must read the same device-ID-scoped timeline web already fetches (web/hooks/useSession + lib/api) through a new useEmotionalState provider in _app.js, using the same weighted-recency + coordinate-fallback logic so web tints match mobile for identical data. Also note stale identity copy: web Layout.js still shows a 'Sign in' nav link and 'until you sign in' hint, but sign-in was removed at v1.0.9 (deviceId is the identity) — must be corrected. No analytics/subscription wiring is implicated by this surface; the Premium tab simply needs to be linked (web/pages/premium.js already exists). No shared palette constant exists in shared/ — both mobile (utils/theme.js, utils/designSystem.js) and web (lib/designSystem.js) maintain their own copies, so color values must be kept in sync manually (they currently agree on the 5 core emotion hues but web lacks the continuous emotionColor function and the AURORA triad map).
reports.summary: Web is roughly 9 versions behind on the visual design system and is missing the entire v1.0.13 'Living Field' atmosphere: there is no emotion-driven AtmosphericField (only 3 fixed-color blurred glow blobs + a single per-page stateGlow), no global useEmotionalState provider, the v1.0.14 promoted icon tab bar is still a stale text-link pill nav (wrong labels/order, missing Premium, stale Sign-in), the font is a different typeface (Trebuchet MS), and EmotionGarden/MoodWeather/StreakOrb plus the Signal radial dial are simplified v1.0.8 versions. Reaching parity is mostly net-new front-end work (animated CSS/SVG atmosphere, an emotional-state provider wired to the existing device-ID timeline, an icon tab bar, and richer component motion) — no new backend is needed, but the atmosphere must be driven from the same device-ID-scoped data mobile uses.

reports.surface: Backend / shared / functions wiring (web lib/api.js + hooks/useSession.js vs mobile services/api.js + useAppSession.js, against backend/pages/api/* and shared/)
reports.mobileBehavior: Mobile is fully DEVICE-ID identity (sign-in removed in v1.0.9). On launch, mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js bootstrap runs a precise sequence keyed by a SecureStore/AsyncStorage deviceId (getOrCreateDeviceId): (1) one-time recover(deviceId, legacyToken) to pull stranded signed-in data, gated by getRecoveryDone; (2) registerDevice(deviceId) to announce install to ops-console; (3) fetchMe(null, deviceId) to hydrate subscription + firstAiFreeAvailable; (4) drain the pending-sync queue (getPendingSyncs -> logMoment(...,lang) -> removePendingSync); (5) getExpoPushToken() then registerPushToken({deviceId,token,platform}); (6) saveNotificationPrefs({daily,weekly,nudge}, null, deviceId) so the backend push-cron honors opt-outs. mobile/services/api.js (REQUEST_TIMEOUT 8s, in-flight GET+POST/logMoment dedup, parses {data}) exposes 24 endpoint wrappers: logMoment(payload,token,lang), editMoment, deleteMomentApi(...,deviceId), recover, fetchTimeline(deviceId,token,+cache-bust _t), fetchWeeklyReport(deviceId,token,lang), fetchMe(token,deviceId), verifySubscription, downloadExport(deviceId,token), fetchHealth, deleteAllData(token,deviceId), registerPushToken, registerDevice, createShareSnapshot(deviceId,token), fetchShareSnapshot(shareToken), unregisterPushToken, saveNotificationPrefs(...,deviceId), getNotificationPrefs(...,deviceId), submitActionFeedback(actionId,response,deviceId,token) -> POST /actions, fetchProgress(token,deviceId), fetchModes(token,deviceId,lang), fetchModeOutput(mode,...), submitModeFeedback(mode,itemId,response,token,deviceId,source) -> POST /modes/feedback, fetchModeProfile, updateModeProfile, regeneratePremium({lang,model,maxWords,style}, 180s timeout). Every anonymous call appends ?deviceId= or sends deviceId in body; reads pass _t cache-buster and lang. localStore.js maintains an offline moment store + a 50-cap pending-anon-sync queue and buildLocalReport (mirrors backend patternEngine for offline). Analytics: analyticsService(.web.js) wraps PostHog (no-op shim on web). Both clients consume @triggermap/shared (constants/emotions, triggers, tags, contributions, premium, notifications, flags; knowledge/movement+nourishment).
reports.webStatus: web/lib/api.js is at v1.0.8: a thin request() (8s timeout, parses {data}) with only 14 wrappers and NO request dedup, NO lang param, NO cache-bust _t. It exposes loginApi/registerApi (email+Google sign-in — a paradigm mobile DELETED) and a sign-in-centric useSession.js (signInWithEmail/registerWithEmail/signInWithGoogle/signOut; isSignedIn = Boolean(user && token)). getDeviceId() uses a DIFFERENT storage key ("triggermap.web.deviceId" via window.localStorage) than mobile's deviceService DEVICE_ID_KEY. Anonymous deviceId is only attached on a few GETs (timeline/weeklyReport/export/progress) and logMoment; editMoment/deleteMoment/deleteAllData send NO deviceId so they 401/400 for anonymous users. submitActionFeedback POSTs to /api/actionFeedback which DOES NOT EXIST (real endpoint: /api/actions). fetchModes/fetchProgress carry no deviceId and report.js only calls loadModes/loadProgress when isSignedIn is true — and since sign-in is gone, isSignedIn is effectively always false, so the premium/progress tabs never fetch. There is NO analytics, NO recover, NO registerDevice, NO push-token, NO notification-prefs (settings.js fakes prefs in localStorage key "tm_notification_prefs"), NO share, NO subscription/verify, NO modes/feedback, NO modes/profile, NO modes/regenerate, NO fetchModeOutput, NO pending-sync queue, NO buildLocalReport offline fallback.

gaps.title: Web is still sign-in identity; mobile is device-ID — entire useSession contract diverged
gaps.description: web/hooks/useSession.js exposes signInWithEmail/registerWithEmail/signInWithGoogle/signOut and treats identity as user+token (isSignedIn). Mobile removed sign-in in v1.0.9: useAppSession.js exposes a stable deviceId with token=null, user=null, plus firstAiFreeAvailable, onboardingComplete, reminder/reflection/nudges flags. Web must be rewritten to a device-ID session: hydrate via fetchMe(null, deviceId), surface subscription + firstAiFreeAvailable, drop login/register/Google. Remove loginApi/registerApi from lib/api.js (and stop importing them). Cascades into report.js/settings.js/premium.js which still branch on isSignedIn / router.push('/login').
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js (bootstrap, value memo lines ~150-260); mobile/services/api.js fetchMe
gaps.webTarget: web/hooks/useSession.js (rewrite), web/lib/api.js (drop loginApi/registerApi), web/pages/login.js (remove or repurpose), web/pages/report.js + settings.js (de-gate isSignedIn)

gaps.title: submitActionFeedback posts to a non-existent endpoint (/api/actionFeedback 404)
gaps.description: web/lib/api.js submitActionFeedback POSTs to /api/actionFeedback — that route does not exist in backend/pages/api. The real endpoint is /api/actions (POST {actionId, response, deviceId}; GET returns {feedback}). All web action feedback silently fails. Repoint to /actions and send deviceId in body+query. Backend normalizes legacy tried/skipped to helped/not_helpful and accepts helped/not_helpful — web must send the helped/not_helpful vocabulary per the feedback-vocabulary memo.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js submitActionFeedback -> POST /actions; backend/pages/api/actions.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js submitActionFeedback

gaps.title: Premium (modes) and progress tabs never fetch — gated behind dead isSignedIn
gaps.description: web/pages/report.js loads progress/modes only `if (... && isSignedIn)`. In the device-ID world isSignedIn is always false, so loadProgress()/loadModes() never run; the premium and progress tabs render empty. Backend /api/progress and /api/modes accept ?deviceId= for anonymous owners. Web fetchProgress/fetchModes also omit deviceId (no token + no deviceId = 400 MISSING_OWNER). Fix: pass deviceId in lib/api.js and remove the isSignedIn gate.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js fetchProgress(token,deviceId)/fetchModes(token,deviceId,lang); backend/pages/api/progress.js, modes/index.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js fetchProgress/fetchModes, web/pages/report.js useEffects (~lines 1354-1365)

gaps.title: Missing endpoints entirely: recover, registerDevice, push-token, notification-prefs, share, subscription/verify, modes/feedback, modes/profile, modes/regenerate, fetchModeOutput, unregisterPushToken, getNotificationPrefs
gaps.description: Mobile wires 24 endpoints; web wires 14, and several of those are wrong. Web is missing wrappers + call sites for: POST /recover (one-time stranded-data recovery), POST /register-device (ops-console install visibility), POST /push-token register/unregister (web should register a Web Push subscription), GET/POST /notification-prefs (web fakes this in localStorage so backend push-cron never sees web opt-outs), POST+GET /share (share-your-week, a v1.0.16 feature — createShareSnapshot/fetchShareSnapshot), POST /subscription/verify (premium purchase), POST /modes/feedback (submitModeFeedback — required for v1.0.15 feedback-that-sticks and the rotating Read actions), GET/PUT /modes/profile, POST /modes/regenerate (premium-first For You), and fetchModeOutput(mode) for per-mode loads. All anonymous variants must send deviceId. Each maps to a real backend route already present under backend/pages/api/.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js (recover, registerDevice, registerPushToken/unregisterPushToken, saveNotificationPrefs/getNotificationPrefs, createShareSnapshot/fetchShareSnapshot, verifySubscription, submitModeFeedback, fetchModeProfile/updateModeProfile, regeneratePremium, fetchModeOutput); backend/pages/api/{recover,register-device,push-token,notification-prefs,share,subscription/verify,modes/feedback,modes/profile,modes/regenerate}.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js (add wrappers), web/hooks/useSession.js (expose), web/pages/report.js + settings.js + premium.js + share/ (call sites)

gaps.title: web/shared is a stale divergent COPY of the monorepo shared/, not the source of truth
gaps.description: web/next.config.js aliases @triggermap/shared to web/shared (a local copy), NOT the monorepo root shared/. The copy is at the v1.0.8 cut: emotions.js and tags.js differ from root (diff -q reports them different), and it is MISSING contributions.js (adaptive contribution tags shipped v1.0.16) and the entire knowledge/ dir (movementLibrary/nourishmentLibrary) plus the contributions export in constants/index.js. Web therefore consumes different emotion-derivation (derivedEmotionLabel/coordinatesToLegacy in EmotionGarden.js) and tag logic than mobile, risking mismatched feeling labels and missing contribution-tag UI. Fix: delete web/shared and re-alias to ../shared (root), or wire the workspace package, then rebuild.
gaps.mobileSource: shared/constants/{emotions,tags,contributions,triggers,premium,notifications,flags}.js, shared/knowledge/*, shared/constants/index.js
gaps.webTarget: web/next.config.js (alias target), web/shared/ (delete), web/pages/index.js, web/pages/timeline.js, web/components/EmotionGarden.js, web/pages/premium.js

gaps.title: No analytics wiring on web (PostHog)
gaps.description: Mobile has analyticsService.js (PostHog) with a .web.js no-op shim used only for the native screenshot build. The real web app has zero analytics — no trackEvent, no PostHog init. If parity includes product analytics, web needs a posthog-js init + trackEvent shim mirroring the mobile event names. Note this is a no-op even on mobile-web by design, so it is the lowest-severity wiring gap.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/analyticsService.js, analyticsService.web.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/analytics.js (create), web/pages/_app.js (init)

gaps.title: logMoment / reads lack lang, cache-bust, and request dedup
gaps.description: Mobile logMoment(payload, token, lang) sends a lang field; fetchWeeklyReport/fetchModes/fetchModeOutput send lang; fetchTimeline/fetchModes append a _t=Date.now() cache-buster; fetchJson dedups concurrent GETs and POST /logMoment. Web logMoment sends only {...payload, deviceId, timestamp} with no lang, and request() has no dedup, no _t. This causes stale cached reads on web and loss of localization, plus duplicate in-flight requests. Backend modes/weeklyReport read lang. Add lang threading + _t + dedup to web/lib/api.js.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js fetchJson dedup (lines 50-73), logMoment lang, fetchTimeline/_t, fetchModes/_t+lang, fetchWeeklyReport lang
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js request()/logMoment/fetchTimeline/fetchWeeklyReport/fetchModes

gaps.title: editMoment/deleteMoment/deleteAllData omit deviceId — break for anonymous (now all) users
gaps.description: Mobile deleteMomentApi(momentId, token, deviceId) and deleteAllData(token, deviceId) append ?deviceId= when there is no token; editMoment relies on the same owner resolution. Web editMomentApi/deleteMomentApi/deleteAllDataApi send NO deviceId and assume a token. Since web will have no token in the device-ID model, these mutate-by-owner calls hit MISSING_OWNER / 401. Backend moment/[id].js and deleteData.js resolve ownerId = user?.id || req.query.deviceId.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/api.js deleteMomentApi, deleteAllData, editMoment; backend/pages/api/moment/[id].js, deleteData.js
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js editMomentApi/deleteMomentApi/deleteAllDataApi (add deviceId query)

gaps.title: deviceId storage key + generation differ from mobile (no cross-surface continuity)
gaps.description: Web getDeviceId() persists under window.localStorage key 'triggermap.web.deviceId' and mints `web-<ts>` when crypto.randomUUID is unavailable; mobile deviceService uses DEVICE_ID_KEY via SecureStore/AsyncStorage. These are intentionally separate identities per surface (a web visit is a distinct device from the phone), so this is not a bug per se — but it means premium/data do NOT follow a user between their phone and the website. If the product intends continuity, recovery/linking is deferred per the device-ID memo; document that web is its own device bucket. No backend mismatch, but worth flagging for product expectations.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/deviceService.js getOrCreateDeviceId (lines 19-45)
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/api.js getDeviceId

gaps.title: No offline pending-sync queue or local report fallback on web
gaps.description: Mobile localStore.js queues failed logMoment payloads (50-cap, retried on next launch) and builds a local weekly report (buildLocalReport) that mirrors the backend patternEngine for offline/cold-start. As an installable PWA, web should provide the equivalent so a moment logged offline is not lost and the report renders before the network returns. Web has neither; logMoment failures are dropped.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/localStore.js (queuePendingSync/getPendingSyncs/removePendingSync, buildLocalReport); mobile/hooks/useAppSession.js drain loop (lines 200-208)
gaps.webTarget: web/lib/localStore.js (create), web/hooks/useSession.js (drain on bootstrap)

gaps.title: Web has a service worker (sw.js) but no Web Push subscription wiring to /push-token
gaps.description: web/public/sw.js + workbox exist (PWA shell) but there is no PushManager subscribe + POST /api/push-token, and no /api/notification-prefs sync. Mobile registers an Expo push token and persists prefs server-side so backend push-cron (backend/pages/api/internal/control/push-cron.js) can reach the device and honor opt-outs. Web's settings.js stores prefs only in localStorage('tm_notification_prefs'), invisible to the backend. For parity, web needs Web Push registration (VAPID) -> /push-token and prefs -> /notification-prefs.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/services/notificationService.js getExpoPushToken; mobile/services/api.js registerPushToken/saveNotificationPrefs; backend/pages/api/push-token.js, notification-prefs.js, internal/control/push-cron.js
gaps.webTarget: web/public/sw.js (push handlers), web/lib/api.js (push-token/notification-prefs wrappers), web/pages/settings.js (replace localStorage prefs)

reports.wiringNotes: Backend is shared: both clients hit the same Next.js API under backend/pages/api/* (logMoment, timeline, weeklyReport, me, moment/[id], deleteData, export, health, progress, actions, modes/{index,feedback,profile,regenerate}, recover, register-device, push-token, notification-prefs, share, subscription/{verify,grant-provisional}, login, register, crash-report). All owner-scoped routes resolve ownerId = validateSession(bearer)?.id || req.query.deviceId || req.body.deviceId, so anonymous device-ID access works IF web sends deviceId — which it largely does NOT for mutations/modes/progress/actions. The biggest correctness mismatch: web posts action feedback to /api/actionFeedback (404; correct route is /api/actions), and web gates modes/progress behind a now-always-false isSignedIn. backend/pages/api/internal/control/* (push-cron, push-schedule, run-job, send-push, clear-cache, manage-user, migrate-auth-devices, pilot-metrics) are ops/admin and batch endpoints, NOT user-facing — confirmed. local-worker/ (batch orchestrator) and ops-console/ are backend-side services that FEED the same backend/Redis (Upstash) the app reads (e.g. push-cron reads notification_prefs:<ownerId>; ops-console reads registerDevice installs). They are correctly NOT web surfaces; the user's 'wired in with backend and local worker and console' means web must hit this same backend with the same device-ID contract so those services see web users. They currently do not: web never calls registerDevice (invisible to ops-console), never persists notification-prefs server-side (push-cron can't honor web opt-outs / can't push to web), and never registers a push token. Identity store: backend keys everything by ownerId in Upstash Redis (redisKey); web uses its own localStorage deviceId ('triggermap.web.deviceId') distinct from mobile's SecureStore DEVICE_ID_KEY — same backend, separate device buckets (acceptable per device-ID memo, recovery deferred). Shared logic: web aliases @triggermap/shared to a STALE local web/shared copy (missing contributions.js + knowledge/, divergent emotions.js/tags.js) instead of the monorepo root shared/, so web does not consume the same emotion model / tags / contribution logic as mobile.
reports.summary: Web's backend wiring is badly behind and partly broken: it still models identity as email/Google sign-in while mobile went fully device-ID at v1.0.9, it posts action feedback to a non-existent /api/actionFeedback route, and it gates the premium/progress fetches behind an isSignedIn flag that is now permanently false — so those tabs never load. It is missing roughly ten endpoints mobile uses (recover, register-device, push-token, notification-prefs, share, subscription/verify, modes feedback/profile/regenerate, fetchModeOutput) plus analytics, the offline sync queue, lang/cache-bust/dedup, and it consumes a stale divergent copy of @triggermap/shared rather than the monorepo source of truth. Bringing web to parity is essentially a rewrite of web/lib/api.js + web/hooks/useSession.js to the device-ID contract, re-aliasing shared/, and wiring the missing routes and Web Push.

reports.mobileBehavior: Mobile is a native Expo/React Native app (app.json v1.0.17), so there is no in-app "install" concept — it is the install target. It defines the brand source-of-truth that the web PWA must mirror: name "TriggerMap", dark theme, splash + adaptive-icon backgroundColor "#0b1220", portrait orientation, scheme "triggermap", package com.triggermap.app. The adaptive (maskable-equivalent) icon is a DEDICATED padded asset (assets/adaptive-icon-padded.png) distinct from the full-bleed icon (assets/icon.png) — i.e. mobile uses a separate safe-zone-padded foreground for masking, whereas web reuses one full-bleed icon for both "any" and "maskable". v1.0.17 added a 6-slide onboarding walkthrough (OnboardingScreen.js / useOnboarding.js: map triggers, 30-sec logging, valence/arousal scale, Timeline, Insights, premium For You) and rewrote the settings GuideModal to drop sign-in/account language (device-ID identity). All user-facing strings ship in en + hi (i18n/en.json, i18n/hi.json).
reports.webStatus: Web (frozen at v1.0.8) is a genuinely installable PWA: manifest.json is valid with name/short_name "TriggerMap", description, start_url "/", scope "/", display "standalone", theme_color + background_color "#0b1220", orientation portrait-primary, and two PNG icons (verified real 192x192 and 512x512 8-bit RGBA). next.config.js wires next-pwa (dest public, register:true, skipWaiting:true, disabled in dev). _app.js injects the manifest link, theme-color, apple-mobile-web-app-capable, black-translucent status bar, viewport-fit=cover, and apple-touch-icon. usePWAInstall.js correctly handles Android beforeinstallprompt (preventDefault + stash + prompt + userChoice) and appinstalled, detects iOS + standalone, and routes iOS to InstallModalIOS.js (Share -> scroll -> Add to Home Screen). Layout.js renders an "Install app" button when canInstall && !isStandalone. globals.css has solid cross-browser handling: env(safe-area-inset-*) padding on .shell and sticky bars, -webkit-backdrop-filter paired with backdrop-filter everywhere, 100dvh/-webkit-fill-available Safari fixes under @supports, 16px input font to block iOS zoom, touch-action:none + native-track range sliders, prefers-reduced-motion, forced-colors, and print rules. So the core install path WORKS on both Android/Chrome and iOS/Safari today.

gaps.title: Maskable icon has no safe-zone padding — Android adaptive mask will crop the logo
gaps.description: manifest.json declares icon-512.png with purpose "any maskable", but icon-512.png / icon.svg are a FULL-BLEED design: a 512x512 rounded-rect (rx 128) filling the entire canvas with the curve + three dots reaching near the edges. Android applies a maskable safe-zone of only the inner ~80% (circle/squircle/rounded mask), so the rounded corners and outer dots get clipped and the icon looks cropped/zoomed on the home screen and in the install dialog. Mobile solves this with a dedicated padded asset (adaptive-icon-padded.png) separate from the full-bleed icon. Web should either (a) ship a separate maskable icon with the artwork inset to ~66% of the canvas on a #0b1220 field, or (b) drop "maskable" from the 512 purpose and add a third dedicated maskable PNG. Reusing one full-bleed asset for both purposes is the bug.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/app.json (android.adaptiveIcon.foregroundImage -> assets/adaptive-icon-padded.png, backgroundColor #0b1220) vs icon -> assets/icon.png
gaps.webTarget: web/public/manifest.json (icons[].purpose), add web/public/icon-maskable-512.png (artwork inset on #0b1220)

gaps.title: theme_color / status-bar color does not match the actual rendered background
gaps.description: manifest theme_color and _app.js <meta theme-color> are both #0b1220, but globals.css body gradient actually starts at #080e1a (top) -> #060a12 -> #040710. On install the Android status bar / splash and the iOS standalone status bar render #0b1220 while the page top paints #080e1a, producing a visible seam at the top of the installed app. Mobile's splash/adaptive backgroundColor is genuinely #0b1220, so the brand value is correct — but the web body background drifted darker. Align them: either set the body top stop to #0b1220, or set theme_color/background_color and the meta to #080e1a so the standalone chrome matches the painted page.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/app.json (splash.backgroundColor / adaptiveIcon.backgroundColor = #0b1220)
gaps.webTarget: web/public/manifest.json (theme_color, background_color), web/pages/_app.js (meta theme-color), web/styles/globals.css (body gradient top stop)

gaps.title: Manifest missing id, lang, dir, categories, screenshots, shortcuts — degraded/duplicated install UX
gaps.description: manifest.json omits: "id" (stable app identity — without it Chrome derives id from start_url; if start_url ever changes the installed app is treated as a new app), "lang"/"dir" (mobile ships en + hi), "categories", "screenshots" + "shortcuts" (Chrome/Edge show a richer install dialog and long-press app-shortcuts when present; without screenshots Android falls back to the minimal mini-infobar instead of the rich install UI). These are not blockers but the install presentation is markedly weaker than it could be. Add "id":"/", "lang":"en", "dir":"ltr", "categories":["health","lifestyle"], screenshots (mobile + wide form factors) and shortcuts to Log/Timeline/Report.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/app.json (name, orientation), mobile/i18n/en.json + hi.json (en+hi support)
gaps.webTarget: web/public/manifest.json

gaps.title: No offline fallback page configured in next-pwa
gaps.description: next.config.js passes only {dest, disable, register, skipWaiting} to next-pwa — no "fallbacks" (e.g. fallbacks: { document: '/_offline' }) and there is no /pages/_offline.js. The generated sw.js uses NetworkFirst for navigations (the 'others' route with a 10s timeout) and precaches only the v1.0.8 page chunks, so a cold navigation to any route while offline (or to a route not in precache) shows the browser's dead-dino error instead of a branded offline screen. For an app whose whole pitch is 'works from any browser / installable', a missing offline document is a real gap. Add an _offline page and wire next-pwa fallbacks; optionally add a navigateFallback.
gaps.mobileSource: n/a (native app caches its own bundle; web must provide an explicit offline fallback)
gaps.webTarget: web/next.config.js (next-pwa fallbacks), create web/pages/_offline.js

gaps.title: Committed sw.js / workbox are stale v1.0.8 build artifacts checked into git
gaps.description: web/public/sw.js and web/public/workbox-4754cb34.js are committed and contain a precache manifest hard-pointing at v1.0.8 hashed chunks (e.g. /_next/static/chunks/pages/index-35707f57aa9ffa7e.js, buildId shQ_PPlA1NgLgwykekMi7). next-pwa regenerates both on every prod build, so a fresh deploy overwrites them and they are harmless at runtime — BUT keeping them in git is a footgun: they reference routes/chunks that no longer match current source (no /share, /legal precaching for the new pages once added), they can cause confusing diffs, and if anyone serves web/public statically without a build the SW would precache 404'd chunks. Add web/public/sw.js + web/public/workbox-*.js to web/.gitignore and remove from tracking.
gaps.mobileSource: n/a
gaps.webTarget: web/.gitignore (add public/sw.js, public/workbox-*.js), untrack web/public/sw.js + web/public/workbox-4754cb34.js

gaps.title: iOS install modal copy is hardcoded English — no i18n parity with mobile (en+hi)
gaps.description: InstallModalIOS.js hardcodes English strings ('Install TriggerMap', 'Tap the Share icon in Safari', 'Add to Home Screen', 'Got it'). Mobile ships every user-facing string in en + hi (i18n/en.json, i18n/hi.json, kept in sync as recently as v1.0.17). A Hindi user on iOS Safari sees English-only install instructions. The instructions are also Safari-specific; iOS Chrome/Edge/Firefox cannot add-to-home-screen at all (only Safari can on iOS), so the modal should ideally note 'open in Safari' when the iOS browser is not Safari — usePWAInstall.js detects iOS by UA but does not distinguish Safari from in-app/Chrome WebViews, so non-Safari iOS users get instructions they cannot follow.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/i18n/en.json, mobile/i18n/hi.json
gaps.webTarget: web/components/InstallModalIOS.js, web/hooks/usePWAInstall.js (Safari-vs-other iOS detection), web i18n layer (if added)

gaps.title: apple-touch-icon uses 192px (Apple expects 180px) and there is no _document for early head injection
gaps.description: _app.js sets <link rel=apple-touch-icon href=/icon-192.png>. Apple's home-screen icon slot is 180x180; supplying 192 works (iOS scales it) but is non-ideal and can soften the icon. Apple also ignores transparency and applies its own rounded mask, so a 180x180 opaque #0b1220-background icon is preferred. Additionally there is no web/pages/_document.js — all PWA/meta tags live in _app.js <Head>, which is fine for most tags but means lang attribute on <html> is unset (defaults, no lang), hurting accessibility/i18n. Add a 180x180 apple-touch-icon and an _document.js with <html lang>.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/app.json (icon assets)
gaps.webTarget: web/pages/_app.js (apple-touch-icon), add web/public/apple-touch-icon-180.png, add web/pages/_document.js

gaps.title: Install affordance only lives in Layout hero — no install nudge in onboarding (web has no onboarding at all)
gaps.description: The only install entry point is the 'Install app' button in Layout.js's hero (rendered when canInstall && !isStandalone). Mobile shipped a 6-slide onboarding walkthrough in v1.0.17 (OnboardingScreen.js, useOnboarding.js) that orients new users; web has no equivalent and therefore no natural place to surface 'install to home screen' during first-run. This is mostly a feature-parity gap (onboarding) but it directly weakens PWA adoption: a first-time mobile-web visitor never sees a prompt to install unless they notice the hero button. Consider surfacing the install CTA / iOS modal as a dismissible first-run nudge once a web onboarding exists.
gaps.mobileSource: mobile/screens/OnboardingScreen.js, mobile/hooks/useOnboarding.js
gaps.webTarget: web/components/Layout.js (or a new web onboarding component), web/hooks/usePWAInstall.js

gaps.title: canInstall is sticky-true on iOS even in non-installable contexts; no dismissal persistence
gaps.description: usePWAInstall.js sets canInstall=true for ALL iOS user agents that are not already standalone, so the 'Install app' button shows for iOS users in webviews/Chrome iOS where Add-to-Home-Screen is unavailable, and it reappears on every visit with no localStorage 'dismissed' memory. On Android the button only appears after beforeinstallprompt fires (correct), but there is likewise no record once dismissed. Minor UX/correctness: gate the iOS path to Safari and persist dismissal so the CTA is not nagging.
gaps.mobileSource: n/a (native handles its own first-run state)
gaps.webTarget: web/hooks/usePWAInstall.js

reports.wiringNotes: The PWA shell itself wires to the backend correctly via the SW runtime routes: sw.js registers a NetworkFirst route for same-origin /api/* (excluding /api/auth/*) with a 10s network timeout and a 16-entry cache — so installed/offline app retains same-origin API behavior. Layout.js calls fetchHealth() (lib/api) for the backend status badge, identical to a browser tab. HOWEVER there is a significant identity-wiring mismatch that bleeds into PWA scope via _app.js: the web app STILL ships full sign-in — _app.js unconditionally loads the Google GSI script (https://accounts.google.com/gsi/client) and login.js implements Google + email/password auth — but mobile removed sign-in in v1.0.9 in favor of device-ID identity (per project memory: deviceId is the identity, premium = depth). The SW even special-cases /api/auth/* (skips caching it), confirming the web still has an auth surface mobile no longer uses. So the installed PWA presents an auth flow and loads a third-party Google script that contradicts the current device-ID model and adds a cross-origin dependency at startup. The manifest scope ('/') correctly contains /login, /share/[token], /legal/*, so all routes stay in-scope and open inside the installed standalone window. No analytics or subscription wiring is touched by the PWA layer itself.
reports.summary: The web PWA is genuinely installable today on both Android/Chrome (beforeinstallprompt flow in usePWAInstall.js) and iOS/Safari (guided InstallModalIOS.js), with valid manifest, real 192/512 icons, registered service worker, and strong safe-area/dvh/backdrop-filter/Safari cross-browser CSS — so the core 'install on any phone' goal is met. The gaps are correctness/polish rather than blockers: the 512 icon is marked maskable but is full-bleed (Android will crop it), theme_color (#0b1220) drifts from the actual page background (#080e1a), there is no offline fallback page, the committed sw.js/workbox are stale v1.0.8 artifacts that should be gitignored, and the iOS modal is English-only while mobile ships en+hi. Separately, the install shell still carries mobile's removed sign-in (Google GSI script in _app.js + login.js), contradicting the v1.0.9 device-ID identity model.
